- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Strict verifier per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3.
2	
3	Per-sentence check enforcing:
4	  (a) at least one provenance token present
5	  (b) every token references an evidence_id in the pool
6	  (c) span bounds are valid (0 <= start <= end <= len(text))
7	  (d) every decimal in the sentence appears in the cited spans
8	  (e) sentence and span share >= MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP content words
9	  (f) I-bug-092: span semantically ENTAILS the sentence's specific claims
10	      (gated behind PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT, defaults off)
11	
12	Checks (a)-(e) are mechanical "provenance hygiene" — they bound a claim to
13	the same TOPIC as its span. Check (f) is "provenance correctness" — it
14	verifies the span actually entails the sentence's specific facts. The
15	audit on 2026-05-09 found that a capable generator can pass (a)-(e) by
16	producing topically-overlapping prose that introduces unsourced specifics
17	(e.g. inserting "pancreatic β-cells", "lipid metabolism", "energy storage"
18	into a span that says only "synergistic actions on insulin secretion,
19	glucagon suppression, appetite regulation, and adipocyte metabolism").
20	
21	Returns a (verifier_pass: bool, drop_reason: str | None) pair, or directly
22	constructs a VerifiedSentence for use by the generator orchestrator.
23	
24	Tunables (read at call time so tests can override):
25	  PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP — minimum shared content words (default 2)
26	  PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT       — "off" | "warn" | "enforce" (default)
27	                                       off:     skip check (f), pre-I-bug-092 behavior
28	                                                — operator override; production runs
29	                                                  ship without provenance-correctness
30	                                                  enforcement
31	                                       warn:    run check (f), log violations,
32	                                                do NOT drop (collect telemetry)
33	                                       enforce: run check (f), drop on
34	                                                NEUTRAL or CONTRADICTED verdict
35	                                                (default per I-bug-095, validated
36	                                                empirically by I-bug-094 live test)
37	  PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL               — entailment judge model
38	                                       (default: google/gemma-4-31b-it,
39	                                        the two-family evaluator)
40	"""
41	
42	from __future__ import annotations
43	
44	import functools
45	import json
46	import logging
47	import os
48	import re
49	
50	from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.provenance import (
51	    ProvenanceToken,
52	    extract_tokens,
53	    get_span_text,
54	    strip_tokens,
55	    validate_token_against_pool,
56	)
57	# I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): CJK / multilingual-aware content tokenization + the
58	# unsegmentable-script fail-closed guard for the strict_verify overlap floor.
59	from src.polaris_graph.generator.script_aware_grounding import (
60	    extra_script_tokens,
61	    has_unsegmentable_content,
62	)
63	from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.verified_report import (
64	    DropReason,
65	    VerifiedSentence,
66	)
67	from src.polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import EvidencePool
68	
69	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
70	
71	
72	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
73	# Tunables
74	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	
76	DEFAULT_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP = 2
77	
78	# Minimal English stopword list. Strict-verify's content-word overlap should
79	# count substantive vocabulary, not function words. This is intentionally
80	# small — the rule is conservative ("at least 2 shared content words")
81	# so even a short stoplist yields the right behavior.
82	_STOPWORDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
83	    "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "but", "if", "of", "in", "on", "at",
84	    "to", "for", "with", "as", "by", "from", "into", "through", "during",
85	    "before", "after", "above", "below", "between", "is", "are", "was",
86	    "were", "be", "been", "being", "have", "has", "had", "having", "do",
87	    "does", "did", "doing", "will", "would", "should", "could", "may",
88	    "might", "must", "can", "this", "that", "these", "those", "it",
89	    "its", "their", "there", "they", "them", "we", "us", "our", "you",
90	    "your", "i", "me", "my", "he", "she", "his", "her",
91	})
92	
93	_DECIMAL_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
94	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_\-]+")
95	# I-deepfix-001 (Wave-2) — PERCENT-role regex mirroring
96	# ``provenance_generator._INTEGER_PERCENT_RE``: capture the number immediately
97	# before "%" or "percent" so a claim's printed percent can be compared
98	# PERCENT-vs-PERCENT against a cited span (never the bare-number union).
99	_PERCENT_RE = re.compile(r"(-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?:%|percent\b)", re.IGNORECASE)
100	
101	
102	def _min_overlap_threshold() -> int:
103	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP", "")
104	    try:
105	        return max(0, int(raw))
106	    except ValueError:
107	        return DEFAULT_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP
108	
109	
110	def _content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
111	    """Lowercase content words (>=3 chars, not stopwords) from `text`.
112	
113	    I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): the Latin path below is byte-identical to the
114	    pre-L4 engine (English unchanged). It is UNIONED with script-aware tokens
115	    (CJK character bigrams + space-delimited non-Latin words) so the
116	    ``overlap_too_low`` floor counts real shared content on non-Latin claims
117	    instead of mis-counting ZERO. Reverts byte-identical under
118	    PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE=0.
119	    """
120	    latin = {
121	        m.group(0).lower()
122	        for m in _WORD_RE.finditer(text)
123	        if len(m.group(0)) >= 3 and m.group(0).lower() not in _STOPWORDS
124	    }
125	    return latin | extra_script_tokens(text)
126	
127	
128	def _decimals(text: str) -> set[str]:
129	    """Numeric tokens (integers + decimals) from `text`."""
130	    return {m.group(0) for m in _DECIMAL_RE.finditer(text)}
131	
132	
133	def _percents(text: str) -> set[str]:
134	    """I-deepfix-001 (Wave-2): PERCENT VALUES printed in `text` — the number
135	    immediately before "%"/"percent" (`_PERCENT_RE` group 1). "15%" and
136	    "15 percent" both yield "15"; a bare "15" (page number / year / count)
137	    yields nothing. Used to require a claim's printed percent to appear AS A
138	    PERCENT in a cited span, not merely as a coincidental bare digit."""
139	    return {m.group(1) for m in _PERCENT_RE.finditer(text)}
140	
141	
142	def _percent_role_match_enabled() -> bool:
143	    """I-deepfix-001 (Wave-2). True (DEFAULT) => every PERCENT value printed in
144	    the sentence must also appear AS A PERCENT in at least one cited span;
145	    otherwise the sentence drops (`numeric_mismatch`-class `percent_not_in_cited_span`).
146	    Strictly faithfulness-TIGHTENING and strictly ADDITIVE — it can only ADD a
147	    drop, never relax the decimal/overlap/entailment checks. Kill-switch
148	    PG_PROVENANCE_PERCENT_ROLE_MATCH=0 reverts BYTE-IDENTICAL. Read at call time."""
149	    v = os.environ.get("PG_PROVENANCE_PERCENT_ROLE_MATCH", "1").strip().lower()
150	    return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")
151	
152	
153	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
154	# P5 (I-deepfix-001) — epistemic-QUALIFIER RETENTION gate
155	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
156	#
157	# Mirrors the Wave-2 PERCENT-role machinery: a strictly ADDITIVE, default-ON gate
158	# with a byte-identical-OFF kill-switch (PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_RETENTION=0).
159	# It can only ADD a ``binding_qualifier_dropped`` drop — it never relaxes an
160	# existing check, so it stacks ON TOP of the I-faith-001 incumbent engine (that
161	# freeze covered engine REPLACEMENT, not an additive strengthening gate).
162	#
163	# WHY: the composer copies a numeral's VALUE but can silently drop the epistemic /
164	# scope qualifier bound to it in the cited span ("some estimates suggest 46% of
165	# jobs ... under a complementary-software scenario" restated as a flat "46% of
166	# jobs"). That is a certainty distortion (From-May-to-Is, arXiv:2606.07951): a
167	# hedged / conditional figure re-stated as a settled fact. The decimal / percent /
168	# overlap legs all PASS the stripped restatement, and the NLI leg is systematically
169	# lenient to hedge-dropping (a "more general" statement is still entailed), so the
170	# defect needs its OWN completeness gate — exactly as the P1-3 numeric-completeness
171	# guard mirrors the numeric leg.
172	#
173	# CALIBRATION (the over-fire guard): the gate anchors on a SUBSTANTIVE numeral
174	# (a decimal with a fractional part, or a percent-expressed value) that is SHARED
175	# between the span and the sentence, and only fires when an epistemic marker sits
176	# within a proximity window of that numeral IN THE SPAN while the sentence carries
177	# NO marker at all. Bare integers (page numbers, years, sample counts like the
178	# box1 negative ``N=1879``) are NOT substantive, so a plain short finding such as
179	# ``HbA1c reduced 2.3 points`` — whose span carries the SAME flat value with no
180	# nearby marker — never fires. The window bounds a marker to the numeral it
181	# actually qualifies; the broad SENTENCE-side marker set means any surviving hedge
182	# lets the sentence pass (the safe under-drop direction).
183	
184	DEFAULT_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS = 12
185	
186	# The four From-May-to-Is (arXiv:2606.07951) epistemic-marker families, curated
187	# for PRECISION (plain factual verbs like "reported"/"indicated"/"expected" are
188	# deliberately excluded from the default so the gate does not over-fire on flat
189	# clinical spans). LAW VI: fully overridable via PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_LEXICON
190	# (comma-separated). Multi-word phrases are matched whitespace-flexibly.
191	_DEFAULT_QUALIFIER_LEXICON: tuple[str, ...] = (
192	    # family 1 — hedges / approximators
193	    "may", "might", "could", "would", "likely", "unlikely", "probably",
194	    "possibly", "possible", "potentially", "perhaps", "approximately",
195	    "about", "around", "roughly", "nearly", "almost", "some", "certain",
196	    "up to", "as many as", "as much as", "at least", "at most",
197	    "no more than", "no fewer than",
198	    # family 2 — non-factive / projection verbs
199	    "estimate", "estimates", "estimated", "suggest", "suggests", "suggested",
200	    "propose", "proposed", "hypothesize", "hypothesized", "predict",
201	    "predicted", "project", "projected", "forecast", "forecasts", "forecasted",
202	    "model", "modeled", "modelled", "assume", "assumed", "believe", "believed",
203	    "claim", "claimed", "appear", "appears", "seem", "seems",
204	    # family 3 — source attribution
205	    "according to", "reportedly", "allegedly", "purportedly", "so-called",
206	    # family 4 — scope / conditional restrictors
207	    "if", "when", "assuming", "provided", "conditional", "scenario",
208	    "hypothetically", "theoretically", "in theory",
209	)
210	
211	
212	def _qualifier_retention_enabled() -> bool:
213	    """I-deepfix-001 (P5). True (DEFAULT) => run the epistemic-qualifier RETENTION
214	    gate; False => skip it. Strictly faithfulness-TIGHTENING and strictly ADDITIVE
215	    (it can only ADD a ``binding_qualifier_dropped`` drop). Kill-switch
216	    PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_RETENTION=0 reverts BYTE-IDENTICAL. Read at call
217	    time so tests can override."""
218	    v = os.environ.get("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_RETENTION", "1").strip().lower()
219	    return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")
220	
221	
222	def _qualifier_proximity_tokens() -> int:
223	    """Proximity window (in whitespace tokens) within which a span marker is bound
224	    to a numeral. PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS (default 12). An
225	    unset / non-integer value falls back to the default; a negative value clamps to
226	    0 (the tightest window). 0 = same-token only."""
227	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS", "").strip()
228	    try:
229	        return max(0, int(raw))
230	    except ValueError:
231	        return DEFAULT_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS
232	
233	
234	def _qualifier_lexicon() -> tuple[str, ...]:
235	    """The active epistemic-marker lexicon. LAW VI: PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_LEXICON
236	    (comma-separated) overrides the curated default; an empty / all-blank override
237	    falls back to the default rather than disabling the gate silently."""
238	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_LEXICON", "").strip()
239	    if not raw:
240	        return _DEFAULT_QUALIFIER_LEXICON
241	    items = tuple(part.strip().lower() for part in raw.split(",") if part.strip())
242	    return items or _DEFAULT_QUALIFIER_LEXICON
243	
244	
245	@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=8)
246	def _qualifier_marker_re(lexicon: tuple[str, ...]) -> "re.Pattern[str]":
247	    """A word-boundary-anchored alternation over `lexicon`, longest phrase first so
248	    a multi-word marker ("up to") wins over its prefix. Internal spaces match any
249	    run of whitespace. Cached per distinct lexicon tuple."""
250	    parts = sorted({m for m in lexicon if m}, key=len, reverse=True)
251	    if not parts:
252	        # An all-blank lexicon can never match — a pattern that matches nothing.
253	        return re.compile(r"(?!x)x")
254	    alts = [re.escape(p).replace(r"\ ", r"\s+").replace(" ", r"\s+") for p in parts]
255	    return re.compile(r"\b(?:" + "|".join(alts) + r")\b", re.IGNORECASE)
256	
257	
258	def _has_epistemic_marker(text: str, marker_re: "re.Pattern[str]") -> bool:
259	    """True iff any epistemic marker appears in `text`."""
260	    return marker_re.search(text) is not None
261	
262	
263	def _substantive_numerals(text: str) -> set[str]:
264	    """Numerals carrying a CLAIMED magnitude: decimals with a fractional part, plus
265	    percent-expressed values (`_percents`). Bare integers — page numbers, years,
266	    sample counts ("N=1879") — are EXCLUDED: they are not the epistemically-hedged
267	    magnitudes the gate protects, and including them would over-fire on plain
268	    findings (the box1 calibration negatives)."""
269	    decimals_with_fraction = {n for n in _decimals(text) if "." in n}
270	    return decimals_with_fraction | _percents(text)
271	
272	
273	def _marker_binds_numeral_in_span(
274	    span_text: str,
275	    numeral: str,
276	    window: int,
277	    marker_re: "re.Pattern[str]",
278	) -> bool:
279	    """True iff an epistemic marker sits within `window` whitespace tokens of an
280	    occurrence of `numeral` inside `span_text`. The numeral is matched as a WHOLE
281	    number within a token ("46" matches "46%," / "46" but not "460" / "46.5"), so a
282	    coincidental substring never binds a marker."""
283	    tokens = span_text.split()
284	    for i, tok in enumerate(tokens):
285	        if numeral not in _decimals(tok):
286	            continue
287	        lo = max(0, i - window)
288	        hi = min(len(tokens), i + window + 1)
289	        if _has_epistemic_marker(" ".join(tokens[lo:hi]), marker_re):
290	            return True
291	    return False
292	
293	
294	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
295	# Entailment judge (I-bug-092 — provenance correctness)
296	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
297	#
298	# Definitions extracted to `polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge` per
299	# I-bug-099 so future call sites have a single canonical home for the
300	# judge class + lazy singleton + telemetry counters. Re-exported here
301	# for backwards compatibility with the existing test pattern that
302	# monkey-patches `strict_verify._get_judge`, `strict_verify._JUDGE_TELEMETRY`,
303	# etc. via `monkeypatch.setattr(strict_verify, ...)`. Both `verify_sentence`
304	# (in this module) and `provenance_generator.verify_sentence_provenance`
305	# (which lazy-imports `_get_judge` from this module) read the re-exported
306	# names — a single rebind on `strict_verify` propagates to both.
307

358	def _entailment_mode() -> str:
359	    """Return one of 'off', 'warn', 'enforce'. Unknown values map to default.
360	
361	    I-bug-097: emit a single WARNING per process per unrecognized typo
362	    string. Empty / unset env returns the default mode silently —
363	    that is the intended default, not a misconfiguration.
364	
365	    I-bug-095: default mode is 'enforce' (was 'off' before this Issue).
366	    Operators who need the pre-graduation behavior can set
367	    PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off explicitly.
368	    """
369	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT", _DEFAULT_MODE).lower().strip()
370	    if raw and raw not in ("off", "warn", "enforce"):
371	        if raw not in _UNKNOWN_MODE_WARNED:
372	            _UNKNOWN_MODE_WARNED.add(raw)
373	            logger.warning(
374	                "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=%r unrecognized; "
375	                "treating as %r (default). Valid: off, warn, enforce.",
376	                raw, _DEFAULT_MODE,
377	            )
378	        return _DEFAULT_MODE
379	    return raw or _DEFAULT_MODE

383	# Per-sentence verifier
384	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
385	
386	def verify_sentence(
387	    sentence_text: str,
388	    pool: EvidencePool,
389	    min_content_overlap: int | None = None,
390	    is_synthesis_claim: bool = False,
391	) -> tuple[bool, DropReason | None]:
392	    """Return (verifier_pass, drop_reason) for `sentence_text` against `pool`.
393	
394	    Pass: returns (True, None).
395	    Fail: returns (False, drop_reason) with reason from DropReason literal.
396	
397	    Implements CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3 in order:
398	      1. at least one well-formed token        → no_provenance_token
399	      2. every token references known source_id → invalid_token
400	      3. spans within source bounds            → span_out_of_range
401	      4. every decimal in sentence in spans    → numeric_mismatch
402	      5. >=N shared content words              → overlap_too_low
403	      6. (I-bug-092) span entails sentence     → entailment_failed
404	         (gated by PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT, off by default)
405	
406	    If `is_synthesis_claim=True` AND the sentence has no tokens, return
407	    (True, None) — synthesis claims pass without provenance by definition
408	    (I-f5-006). If a synthesis claim DOES carry tokens, that's a generator
409	    bug; we still run token checks so the underlying invariant holds.
410	    """
411	    threshold = (
412	        min_content_overlap
413	        if min_content_overlap is not None
414	        else _min_overlap_threshold()
415	    )
416	
417	    tokens = extract_tokens(sentence_text)
418	    if not tokens:
419	        if is_synthesis_claim:
420	            return True, None
421	        return False, "no_provenance_token"
422	
423	    # Validate each token against the pool. Collect span texts for
424	    # later numeric + overlap checks.
425	    span_texts: list[str] = []
426	    for token in tokens:
427	        reason = validate_token_against_pool(token, pool)
428	        if reason == "invalid_token":
429	            return False, "invalid_token"
430	        if reason == "span_out_of_range":
431	            return False, "span_out_of_range"
432	        # Token valid — fetch its span text
433	        span = get_span_text(token, pool)
434	        if span is None:
435	            # Belt-and-suspenders: token validated but get_span_text said no.
436	            return False, "span_out_of_range"
437	        span_texts.append(span)
438	
439	    sentence_clean = strip_tokens(sentence_text)
440	    combined_span = " ".join(span_texts)
441	
442	    # Decimal match: every decimal in sentence must appear in the combined
443	    # span text. Permits the spans to contain MORE decimals than the
444	    # sentence cites — that's fine; the constraint is one-way.
445	    sentence_decimals = _decimals(sentence_clean)
446	    span_decimals = _decimals(combined_span)
447	    if not sentence_decimals.issubset(span_decimals):
448	        return False, "numeric_mismatch"
449	
450	    # I-deepfix-001 (Wave-2) PERCENT-ROLE re-check (parity with
451	    # provenance_generator.verify_sentence_provenance). A printed percent
452	    # ("15%", "15 percent") is a PERCENT claim: it must be carried by a cited
453	    # span AS A PERCENT, not merely as a bare digit that coincidentally equals
454	    # the value (a page number / year / count). The decimal check above passes a
455	    # bare "15" via the number union; this compares PERCENT-vs-PERCENT
456	    # (`_percents`, same regex both sides). Span percents are read PER cited span
457	    # text (not the joined string) so a "15" ending one span and a "%" starting
458	    # the next can never be glued into a spurious percent. STRICTLY ADDITIVE:
459	    # only ADDS a drop; nothing grounded by a genuine in-span percent is newly
460	    # dropped. Default-ON; PG_PROVENANCE_PERCENT_ROLE_MATCH=0 reverts byte-identical.
461	    if _percent_role_match_enabled():
462	        sentence_percents = _percents(sentence_clean)
463	        if sentence_percents:
464	            span_percents: set[str] = set()
465	            for _span in span_texts:
466	                span_percents |= _percents(_span)
467	            if not sentence_percents.issubset(span_percents):
468	                return False, "percent_not_in_cited_span"
469	
470	    # I-deepfix-001 (P5) EPISTEMIC-QUALIFIER RETENTION re-check. The composer can
471	    # copy a numeral's VALUE but drop the hedge / scope qualifier the cited span
472	    # binds to it ("some estimates suggest 46% ... under a complementary-software
473	    # scenario" -> a flat "46%"). For each cited span, if a SUBSTANTIVE numeral in
474	    # the span (decimal / percent — never a bare count / year) also appears in the
475	    # sentence AND an epistemic marker sits within
476	    # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS of THAT numeral IN THE SPAN, the
477	    # sentence must itself carry a marker; else it dropped a binding qualifier ->
478	    # (False, "binding_qualifier_dropped"). Span percents/decimals are read PER
479	    # cited span (not the joined string) so a marker in one span can never bind a
480	    # numeral in another. STRICTLY ADDITIVE: only ADDS a drop; the verbatim K-span
481	    # fallback retains the qualifier by construction, so a genuine hedged finding
482	    # still ships. Default-ON; PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_RETENTION=0 reverts
483	    # byte-identical. Read at call time.
484	    if _qualifier_retention_enabled():
485	        marker_re = _qualifier_marker_re(_qualifier_lexicon())
486	        if not _has_epistemic_marker(sentence_clean, marker_re):
487	            window = _qualifier_proximity_tokens()
488	            sentence_numbers = _decimals(sentence_clean)
489	            for _span in span_texts:
490	                shared = _substantive_numerals(_span) & sentence_numbers
491	                for numeral in shared:
492	                    if _marker_binds_numeral_in_span(
493	                        _span, numeral, window, marker_re
494	                    ):
495	                        return False, "binding_qualifier_dropped"
496	
497	    # I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): FAIL-CLOSED on unsegmentable script content. A
498	    # claim carrying a run of letters in a script we cannot segment
499	    # (Thai/Lao/Khmer/Myanmar/Tibetan) yields no content tokens; rather than let
500	    # it slip past the overlap floor on a coincidental decimal match, we drop it
501	    # — we cannot establish lexical grounding and MUST NOT guess a pass (the
502	    # lethal weakened-positive). CJK / Arabic / Cyrillic etc. tokenize correctly
503	    # via _content_words and never reach here. Reverts byte-identical under
504	    # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE=0.
505	    if has_unsegmentable_content(sentence_clean):
506	        return False, "unsegmentable_script"
507	
508	    # Content-word overlap
509	    sentence_words = _content_words(sentence_clean)
510	    span_words = _content_words(combined_span)
511	    # BUG-03 (FX-02, #1106): a truly contentless sentence — NO content words AND no decimals —
512	    # is dropped EXPLICITLY here (mirrors the provenance_generator floor). With the default
513	    # threshold=2 it would already fail as overlap_too_low, but this fail-closes the
514	    # PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP=0 edge (where overlap=0 is not < 0) and gives a precise
515	    # reason. Faithfulness-TIGHTENING: any content word OR decimal routes to the numeric/overlap
516	    # floors, so a real clinical sentence can never trip this.
517	    if not sentence_words and not sentence_decimals:
518	        return False, "empty_or_contentless_sentence"
519	    overlap = len(sentence_words & span_words)
520	    if overlap < threshold:
521	        return False, "overlap_too_low"
522	
523	    # Check (f) — entailment judge (I-bug-092). Synthesis claims with no
524	    # tokens already short-circuited above; if a synthesis claim DOES
525	    # carry tokens it must clear the same content-correctness bar as
526	    # any other cited sentence.
527	    mode = _entailment_mode()
528	    if mode in ("warn", "enforce"):
529	        verdict, reason = _get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span)
530	        _record_judge_outcome(verdict, reason)
531	        # I-ready-002 (#1071) P0: the judge FAILS OPEN to ("ENTAILED","judge_error:...") on an API/parse
532	        # error. Counting that as a genuine ENTAILED ships an UNVERIFIED clinical claim as "verified" — a
533	        # silent downgrade of the binding faithfulness gate (LAW II / lethal-in-clinical). Detect it and
534	        # FAIL CLOSED in enforce mode (drop the sentence, like an unsupported one); warn-mode logs only.
535	        if isinstance(reason, str) and reason.startswith("judge_error:"):
536	            # I-deepfix-001 W11-section-verify-judge-error-degrade (#1344): a judge_error is
537	            # a TRANSPORT fault (the judge socket failed / returned unparseable), NOT a
538	            # genuine NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED faithfulness verdict. The mechanical provenance
539	            # gates (a)-(e) ALREADY passed above (evidence-id, span-bounds, decimals, >=2
540	            # content-word overlap), so this is an otherwise span-grounded claim being
541	            # dropped purely because of a socket fault. Under a SUSTAINED judge outage the
542	            # enforce-drop can empty EVERY section -> abort_no_verified_sections despite real
543	            # span-grounded evidence. Per the operator-locked "verifier NEVER holds a report":
544	            # when PG_STRICT_VERIFY_JUDGE_ERROR_ALWAYS_RELEASE is ON, DEGRADE to KEEP-with-
545	            # disclosed-label (the per-claim 'entailment_unverified_judge_error' label IS the
546	            # faithfulness signal). This applies ONLY to judge_error (transport) — genuine
547	            # NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED below stay DROPPED (faithfulness NOT relaxed). Default-OFF
548	            # preserves the byte-identical enforce-drop.
549	            logger.warning(
550	                "entailment judge_error (mode=%s): sentence=%r reason=%r — failing closed",
551	                mode, sentence_clean, reason,
552	            )
553	            if mode == "enforce":
554	                _je_release = os.environ.get(
555	                    "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_JUDGE_ERROR_ALWAYS_RELEASE", "0",
556	                ).strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
557	                if _je_release:
558	                    logger.warning(
559	                        "entailment judge_error (mode=enforce): KEEPING span-grounded "
560	                        "sentence with disclosed label (always-release; transport fault, "
561	                        "not a NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED verdict): %r", sentence_clean,
562	                    )
563	                    return True, "entailment_unverified_judge_error"
564	                return False, "entailment_judge_error_fail_closed"
565	        if verdict in ("NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED"):
566	            logger.warning(
567	                "entailment %s (mode=%s): sentence=%r reason=%r",
568	                verdict, mode, sentence_clean, reason,
569	            )
570	            if mode == "enforce":
571	                return False, "entailment_failed"
572	
573	    return True, None
574	
575	
576	def verify_sentence_to_record(
577	    sentence_text: str,
578	    section_id: str,
579	    pool: EvidencePool,
580	    min_content_overlap: int | None = None,
581	    is_synthesis_claim: bool = False,
582	) -> VerifiedSentence:
583	    """Convenience: wrap verify_sentence into a VerifiedSentence record.
584	
585	    Used by the generator orchestrator to build Section.verified_sentences.
586	    """
587	    passed, reason = verify_sentence(
588	        sentence_text,
589	        pool,
590	        min_content_overlap=min_content_overlap,
591	        is_synthesis_claim=is_synthesis_claim,
592	    )
593	    tokens = [t.raw for t in extract_tokens(sentence_text)]
594	    # I-deepfix-001 (Codex e2e gate P1): verify_sentence can return (passed=True, reason=<label>)
595	    # for the judge-error always-release path (span-grounded sentence KEPT with a disclosed
596	    # caveat). The VerifiedSentence schema forbids a non-None drop_reason when verifier_pass=True,
597	    # so a KEPT-with-disclosure caveat MUST ride in release_disclosure, not drop_reason — else the
598	    # construction raises and the intended ship-with-label ABORTS. drop_reason stays the
599	    # DROPPED-only reason (passed is False). Faithfulness untouched: NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED still
600	    # fail (passed=False) and carry drop_reason exactly as before.
601	    kept_disclosure_label = reason if (passed and reason is not None) else None
602	    drop_reason = None if passed else reason
603	    return VerifiedSentence(
604	        section_id=section_id,
605	        sentence_text=sentence_text,
606	        provenance_tokens=tokens,
607	        verifier_pass=passed,
608	        drop_reason=drop_reason,
609	        kept_disclosure_label=kept_disclosure_label,
610	        evaluator_agrees=passed,
611	        is_synthesis_claim=is_synthesis_claim,
612	    )
613

616	# Section-level rollup
617	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
618	
619	def section_pass_rate(sentences: list[VerifiedSentence]) -> float:
620	    """Fraction of sentences that passed verify, in [0.0, 1.0].
621	
622	    Empty list returns 0.0 (vacuously below any threshold).
623	    """
624	    if not sentences:
625	        return 0.0
626	    passed = sum(1 for s in sentences if s.verifier_pass)
627	    return passed / len(sentences)

1	"""VerifiedReport schema for slice 003 (generator + strict-verify).
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_003/architecture_proposal.md` §"verified_report.py".
4	
5	Pure-types module. No I/O, no LLM, no network. Slice 003's
6	process_generation() consumes an EvidencePool (slice 002 output) and
7	produces a VerifiedReport, which slice 004 will export as an audit
8	bundle.
9	
10	Lives in `polaris_graph.clinical_generator` to keep separation
11	from heritage `polaris_graph.generator` (multi_section, live_deepseek)
12	which serves the legacy honest-rebuild pipeline. Coexistence by design
13	per PLAN.md §4 Heritage Import.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import uuid
19	from datetime import datetime, timezone
20	from typing import Literal
21	
22	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator, model_validator
23	
24	
25	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	# Type literals
27	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	
29	SectionStatus = Literal["verified", "regenerated", "dropped"]
30	"""Per-section outcome from strict_verify:
31	
32	- "verified": original generation passed >=verifier_pass_threshold of
33	  sentences strict-verified
34	- "regenerated": original failed; regeneration attempt passed
35	- "dropped": both attempts failed; section excluded from final report
36	"""
37	
38	PipelineVerdict = Literal["success", "abort_no_verified_sections"]
39	"""Top-level verdict per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 4."""
40	
41	AssertionSurface = Literal[
42	    "prose",
43	    "table",
44	    "summary_bullet",
45	    "limitation",
46	    "caption",
47	    "heading",
48	]
49	"""Render-surface kind of a verified assertion (I-f5-009).
50	
51	Per Carney plan §F: every assertion-bearing surface in a verified report
52	must be gated-and-clickable through the Inspector OR explicitly marked
53	ungated. Today's generator emits only "prose"; future Issues populate
54	the other 5 surfaces as the generator learns to label table cells,
55	summary bullets, etc."""
56	
57	DropReason = Literal[
58	    "invalid_token",       # token doesn't reference a known source_id
59	    "span_out_of_range",   # span_start > span_end or > len(full_text)
60	    "numeric_mismatch",    # decimal in sentence not present in cited span
61	    "overlap_too_low",     # < min_content_overlap shared content words
62	    "no_provenance_token", # sentence has no [#ev:...:start-end] tokens
63	    "entailment_failed",   # I-bug-092: span does not entail sentence (semantic check)
64	    "empty_or_contentless_sentence",  # BUG-03/FX-02 (#1106): no content words AND no decimals
65	    "percent_not_in_cited_span",  # I-deepfix-001 Wave-2: a printed percent (N%) not carried AS A PERCENT by any cited span
66	    "binding_qualifier_dropped",  # I-deepfix-001 P5: span binds an epistemic/scope marker to a shared numeral but the sentence dropped every marker (certainty distortion)
67	    "unsegmentable_script",  # I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): claim carries an unsegmentable non-Latin script (Thai/Lao/Khmer/Myanmar/Tibetan) — grounding cannot be established, fail-closed
68	]

75	class VerifiedSentence(BaseModel):
76	    """A single sentence emitted by the generator after strict-verify.
77	
78	    Sentences that fail strict_verify are still represented here with
79	    `verifier_pass=False` and a `drop_reason`, so the audit bundle can
80	    surface "what got proposed and dropped" alongside "what shipped."
81	    """
82	
83	    section_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
84	    sentence_text: str = Field(min_length=1)
85	    provenance_tokens: list[str] = Field(
86	        default_factory=list,
87	        description="Token strings of form '[#ev:<source_id>:<start>-<end>]'",
88	    )
89	    verifier_pass: bool
90	    drop_reason: DropReason | None = None
91	    kept_disclosure_label: str | None = Field(
92	        default=None,
93	        description=(
94	            "I-deepfix-001 (Codex e2e gate P1): a per-sentence KEPT-with-disclosure caveat for a "
95	            "sentence that PASSED strict-verify but carries a release note (e.g. "
96	            "'entailment_unverified_judge_error' = span-grounded yet the entailment judge was "
97	            "unreachable; kept-and-disclosed per the operator-locked 'verifier never holds a "
98	            "report' rule). drop_reason MUST stay None when verifier_pass=True, so this caveat "
99	            "rides HERE, not in drop_reason. NOTE: distinct from the run-level manifest key "
100	            "'release_disclosure' (a dict for D8 unadjudicated disclosure) — this is per-sentence. "
101	            "Default None preserves byte-identical back-compat."
102	        ),
103	    )
104	    evaluator_agrees: bool | None = Field(
105	        default=None,
106	        description=(
107	            "Two-family evaluator agreement per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 1. "
108	            "True = evaluator confirms generator claim; False = evaluator "
109	            "disagrees; None = pending (no evaluator pass yet). At rule-based "
110	            "stage this mirrors verifier_pass; future Issue plugs in the "
111	            "real two-family LLM judge to populate this independently."
112	        ),
113	    )
114	    assertion_surface: AssertionSurface = Field(
115	        default="prose",
116	        description=(
117	            "Render-surface kind per I-f5-009. Default 'prose' preserves "
118	            "back-compat with all existing fixtures. Non-prose values are "
119	            "populated by future generator wiring (table cells, summary "
120	            "bullets, limitations, captions, headings); the Inspector gates "
121	            "all six surfaces equally."
122	        ),
123	    )
124	    evaluator_disagreement: "EvaluatorDisagreement | None" = Field(
125	        default=None,
126	        description=(
127	            "Optional detail when evaluator_agrees=False (I-f9-002). "
128	            "Carries the evaluator's competing reading of the same evidence "
129	            "so the EvaluatorPane can render generator-vs-evaluator side-by-side."
130	        ),
131	    )
132	    contradiction: "ContradictionSignal | None" = Field(
133	        default=None,
134	        description=(
135	            "Optional contradiction signal (I-f8-001). When present, "
136	            "≥2 sources cited for this sentence disagree on the claim. "
137	            "Default None preserves back-compat. Future Issue (F8-002+) "
138	            "wires real detection into the generator."
139	        ),
140	    )
141	    is_synthesis_claim: bool = Field(

143	        description=(
144	            "True iff sentence is a synthesis claim (e.g., a discussion "
145	            "paragraph summarizing across sources without quoting any one). "
146	            "Synthesis claims have no provenance_tokens by definition; "
147	            "non-synthesis kept sentences MUST have ≥1 provenance token. "
148	            "Today's generator defaults to False; future Issue may wire the "

150	        ),

153	    @model_validator(mode="after")
154	    def _drop_reason_consistency(self) -> "VerifiedSentence":
155	        if self.verifier_pass is False and self.drop_reason is None:
156	            raise ValueError(
157	                "drop_reason is required when verifier_pass=False"
158	            )

160	            raise ValueError(

162	            )

170	                "(rule-based dropped the sentence; evaluator cannot pass it "

172	            )

174	
175	    @model_validator(mode="after")
176	    def _synthesis_claim_consistency(self) -> "VerifiedSentence":
177	        if self.is_synthesis_claim and self.verifier_pass is False:
178	            raise ValueError(
179	                "is_synthesis_claim=True requires verifier_pass=True "
180	                "(synthesis claims either ship or are dropped before reaching "
181	                "this record)"
182	            )
183	        if self.is_synthesis_claim and len(self.provenance_tokens) > 0:
184	            raise ValueError(
185	                "is_synthesis_claim=True requires provenance_tokens=[] "
186	                "(synthesis claims have no specific provenance — that's the "
187	                "definition)"
188	            )
189	        if (
190	            not self.is_synthesis_claim
191	            and self.verifier_pass is True
192	            and len(self.provenance_tokens) == 0
193	        ):
194	            raise ValueError(
195	                "kept non-synthesis sentence requires ≥1 provenance token; "
196	                "set is_synthesis_claim=True for prose without specific "
197	                "provenance"
198	            )
199	        return self
200	
201	
202	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
203	# FrameCoverage (I-f7-001)
204	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
205	
206	Jurisdiction = Literal[
207	    "canada",
208	    "us",
209	    "eu",
210	    "uk",
211	    "who",
212	    "other",
213	    "unspecified",
214	]
215	"""I-f8-006 jurisdiction tag on a contradiction side.
216	
217	Surfaces regulator/jurisdiction (FDA-US vs Health-Canada vs EMA-EU vs MHRA-UK
218	vs WHO) so cross-jurisdiction disagreements render explicitly."""
219	
220	
221	ContradictionEvidenceType = Literal[
222	    "trial",
223	    "guideline",
224	    "meta_analysis",
225	    "observational",
226	    "regulatory_label",
227	    "expert_opinion",
228	    "unspecified",
229	]
230	"""I-f8-005 evidence-type tag on a contradiction side.
231	
232	Distinguishes guideline-vs-trial, regulatory-vs-trial, etc. — common
233	sources of "the trial says X but the guideline says Y" contradictions."""
234	
235	
236	class EvaluatorDisagreement(BaseModel):
237	    """Two-family evaluator disagreement detail (I-f9-002).
238	
239	    Carries both readings of the same evidence so the user can compare
240	    the generator's claim against the evaluator's competing reading."""
241	
242	    generator_reading: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=1000)
243	    evaluator_reading: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=1000)
244	    cited_sources: list[str] = Field(min_length=1)
245	    evaluator_model: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
246	
247	
248	class ContradictionSide(BaseModel):
249	    """One side of a contradiction (I-f8-002).
250	
251	    Each side is a distinct cited source's position on the claim. Tier
252	    + sample size + hedge language + PT08 flag inform reviewer judgment
253	    of which side has the stronger evidentiary backing. evidence_type
254	    (I-f8-005) tags the kind of source (trial, guideline, ...)."""
255	
256	    source_id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
257	    source_tier: Literal["T1", "T2", "T3"]
258	    sample_size: int | None = Field(default=None, ge=0)
259	    hedge_language: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
260	    pt08_flag: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=50)
261	    claim_excerpt: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=500)
262	    evidence_type: ContradictionEvidenceType = Field(default="unspecified")
263	    jurisdiction: Jurisdiction = Field(default="unspecified")
264	
265	
266	ContradictionCategory = Literal[
267	    "numeric",
268	    "categorical",
269	    "regulatory",
270	    "temporal",
271	    "jurisdictional",
272	    "other",
273	]
274	"""I-f8-004 contradiction-category enum.
275	
276	Distinguishes the kind of disagreement: numeric (dose/CI), categorical
277	(approved/not approved), regulatory (FDA vs EMA), temporal (pre-2020 vs
278	post-2020 evidence shift), jurisdictional (Canada vs US), other."""
279	
280	
281	ContradictionKind = Literal["multi_source", "self_contradiction"]
282	"""I-f8-003 contradiction-kind discriminator.
283	
284	`multi_source`: ≥2 distinct sources disagree (I-f8-001/002 case).
285	`self_contradiction`: 1 source contradicts itself across spans/paragraphs."""
286	
287	
288	class ContradictionSignal(BaseModel):
289	    """Inline contradiction annotation per sentence (I-f8-001).
290	
291	    Indicates ≥2 cited sources disagree on the claim in the sentence.
292	    `sides` (added I-f8-002) carries the per-source detail consumed by
293	    the ContradictionPane; empty preserves I-f8-001 back-compat.
294	    `kind` (added I-f8-003) discriminates multi-source vs self-contradiction."""
295	
296	    disagreeing_source_count: int = Field(ge=1, le=20)
297	    summary: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=500)
298	    sides: list[ContradictionSide] = Field(default_factory=list)
299	    kind: ContradictionKind = Field(default="multi_source")
300	    category: ContradictionCategory = Field(default="other")
301	
302	    @model_validator(mode="after")
303	    def _kind_count_consistency(self) -> "ContradictionSignal":
304	        if self.kind == "multi_source":
305	            if self.disagreeing_source_count < 2:
306	                raise ValueError(
307	                    "kind='multi_source' requires "
308	                    "disagreeing_source_count >= 2"
309	                )
310	            if (
311	                self.sides
312	                and len(self.sides) != self.disagreeing_source_count
313	            ):
314	                raise ValueError(
315	                    "len(sides) must equal disagreeing_source_count for "
316	                    "multi_source when sides is non-empty"
317	                )
318	        else:  # self_contradiction
319	            if self.disagreeing_source_count != 1:
320	                raise ValueError(
321	                    "kind='self_contradiction' requires "
322	                    "disagreeing_source_count == 1"
323	                )
324	            if len(self.sides) < 2:
325	                raise ValueError(
326	                    "kind='self_contradiction' requires sides with ≥2 "
327	                    "entries representing distinct contradicting spans "
328	                    "from the same source"
329	                )
330	            distinct_source_ids = {s.source_id for s in self.sides}
331	            if len(distinct_source_ids) != 1:
332	                raise ValueError(
333	                    "kind='self_contradiction' requires all sides to "
334	                    "reference the same source_id"
335	                )
336	        return self
337	
338	
339	GapReason = Literal[
340	    "paywalled",
341	    "no_oa",
342	    "source_tier_ineligible",
343	    "language_unavailable",
344	    "retracted_only",
345	    "jurisdiction_outside",
346	    "not_indexed",
347	    "embargoed",
348	    "other",
349	]
350	"""Frozen taxonomy of frame-gap reasons (I-f7-002).
351	
352	Replaces the free-text reason from I-f7-001. Forces consistent UI
353	labels and analytics aggregation. `other` is the escape hatch — paired
354	with `reason_detail` when it's used."""
355	
356	
357	class FrameGap(BaseModel):
358	    """A single uncovered entity in a frame coverage report."""
359	
360	    entity_name: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
361	    reason: GapReason
362	    reason_detail: str | None = Field(
363	        default=None,
364	        max_length=500,
365	        description=(
366	            "Optional free-text supplement to the enum reason "
367	            "(I-f7-002). Renders as 'Label: detail' in the panel."
368	        ),
369	    )
370	
371	
372	class FrameCoverage(BaseModel):
373	    """Top-of-report frame coverage summary (I-f7-001).
374	
375	    Surfaces "X of N entities covered" with explicit gap reasons. Consumed
376	    by the FrameCoveragePanel above the verified report. Optional on
377	    VerifiedReport; older payloads without coverage render no panel."""
378	
379	    covered_entity_count: int = Field(ge=0)
380	    total_entity_count: int = Field(ge=0)
381	    gaps: list[FrameGap] = Field(default_factory=list)
382	
383	    @model_validator(mode="after")
384	    def _counts_consistency(self) -> "FrameCoverage":
385	        if self.covered_entity_count > self.total_entity_count:
386	            raise ValueError(
387	                "covered_entity_count cannot exceed total_entity_count"
388	            )
389	        if (
390	            self.covered_entity_count + len(self.gaps)
391	            != self.total_entity_count
392	        ):
393	            raise ValueError(
394	                "covered_entity_count + len(gaps) must equal total_entity_count"
395	            )
396	        return self
397	
398	
399	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
400	# Section
401	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
402	
403	class Section(BaseModel):
404	    """A report section (e.g. 'Population', 'Outcomes').
405	
406	    `verified_sentences` includes both passed AND dropped sentences for
407	    audit completeness; UI filters by `verifier_pass` when rendering the
408	    user-facing report. `section_verify_pass_rate` is over all attempted
409	    sentences (not just kept ones).
410	    """
411	
412	    section_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
413	    section_title: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
414	    verified_sentences: list[VerifiedSentence] = Field(default_factory=list)
415	    section_verify_pass_rate: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
416	    section_status: SectionStatus
417	
418	    def kept_sentences(self) -> list[VerifiedSentence]:
419	        """Filter to sentences that passed strict_verify."""
420	        return [s for s in self.verified_sentences if s.verifier_pass]
421	
422	
423	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
424	# VerifiedReport
425	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
426	
427	class VerifiedReport(BaseModel):
428	    """The slice 003 output. Bound to a slice-002 pool via pool_id +
429	    a slice-001 decision via decision_id. Same pool can produce different
430	    reports across runs (LLM nondeterminism); report_id distinguishes them.
431	    """
432	
433	    report_id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
434	    pool_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
435	    decision_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
436	    sections: list[Section] = Field(default_factory=list)
437	    overall_verify_pass_rate: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
438	    pipeline_verdict: PipelineVerdict
439	    generator_model: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
440	    evaluator_model: str = Field(
441	        min_length=1,
442	        max_length=200,
443	        description=(
444	            "Two-family evaluator identifier per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 1. "
445	            "Rule-based stage value: 'strict_verify_v1'. When a real LLM "
446	            "judge wires in (separate Issue), this becomes the OpenRouter "
447	            "model id from a different training family than generator_model."
448	        ),
449	    )
450	    family_segregation_passed: bool = Field(
451	        default=True,
452	        description=(
453	            "True iff generator and evaluator are confirmed to be from "
454	            "different training lineages (rule-based always True; future "
455	            "two-family LLM judge invokes "
456	            "openrouter_client.check_family_segregation)."
457	        ),
458	    )
459	    verifier_pass_threshold: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
460	    started_at_utc: datetime
461	    finished_at_utc: datetime
462	    latency_ms: int = Field(ge=0)
463	    cost_usd: float = Field(ge=0.0)
464	    frame_coverage: FrameCoverage | None = Field(
465	        default=None,
466	        description=(
467	            "Optional top-of-report frame coverage summary (I-f7-001). "
468	            "When present, the Inspector renders a panel above-the-fold "
469	            "showing 'X of N entities covered' + each gap with reason. "
470	            "Default None preserves back-compat with older payloads."
471	        ),
472	    )
473	
474	    @field_validator("finished_at_utc")
475	    @classmethod
476	    def _finished_after_started(cls, v: datetime, info) -> datetime:
477	        started = info.data.get("started_at_utc")
478	        if started is not None and v < started:
479	            raise ValueError(
480	                "finished_at_utc must be >= started_at_utc"
481	            )
482	        return v
483	
484	    @model_validator(mode="after")
485	    def _verdict_consistency(self) -> "VerifiedReport":
486	        """If verdict=success, at least one section must be verified or
487	        regenerated. If verdict=abort_no_verified_sections, every section
488	        (if any) must be 'dropped' or sections may be empty."""
489	        if self.pipeline_verdict == "success":
490	            kept = [
491	                s for s in self.sections if s.section_status != "dropped"
492	            ]
493	            if not kept:
494	                raise ValueError(
495	                    "pipeline_verdict='success' requires at least one "
496	                    "non-dropped section"
497	                )
498	        else:  # abort_no_verified_sections
499	            for section in self.sections:
500	                if section.section_status != "dropped":
501	                    raise ValueError(
502	                        "pipeline_verdict='abort_no_verified_sections' "
503	                        "requires all sections (if any) to be dropped"
504	                    )
505	        return self
506	
507	    def kept_sections(self) -> list[Section]:
508	        """Filter to non-dropped sections."""
509	        return [s for s in self.sections if s.section_status != "dropped"]
510	
511	
512	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
513	# GenerationError (parallel to slice 001 IntakeError + slice 002 RetrievalError)
514	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
515	
516	class GenerationError(BaseModel):
517	    """Returned when generation cannot run (e.g. inadequate input pool)."""
518	
519	    error: bool = True
520	    code: str  # 'inadequate_pool' | 'completion_backend_unavailable' | 'malformed_output'
521	    message: str
522	    pool_id: str | None = None
523	    decision_id: str | None = None
