1	"""Clinical retriever orchestrator — slice 002 main entry.
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_002/architecture_proposal.md` §"clinical_retriever".
4	
5	Pipeline:
6	    ScopeDecision (slice 001 in_scope clinical_*)
7	        ↓
8	    plan_queries()                    -> list[str]
9	        ↓
10	    fetch_fn(query) for each query    -> list[FetchResult]
11	        ↓
12	    classify_url() drops + tiers      -> list[Source]
13	        ↓
14	    de-duplicate by canonical URL     -> list[Source]
15	        ↓
16	    template_for_scope_class()        -> ClinicalTemplate
17	        ↓
18	    assess()                          -> AdequacyVerdict
19	        ↓
20	    EvidencePool
21	
22	Network-free by design: callers inject fetch_fn (FetchHttpFn protocol)
23	that returns deterministic FetchResults. The default fetcher raises
24	NotImplementedError so unit tests + golden tests cannot accidentally
25	make network calls. PR 7 will ship a real Serper + Semantic Scholar
26	backed fetch_fn behind the same Protocol.
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import time
32	from dataclasses import dataclass
33	from datetime import datetime, timezone
34	from typing import Iterable, Protocol
35	
36	from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.clinical_source_registry import classify_url
37	from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.corpus_adequacy_gate import (
38	    ClinicalTemplate,
39	    assess,
40	    template_for_scope_class,
41	)
42	from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import (
43	    EvidencePool,
44	    RetrievalError,
45	    Source,
46	    SourceTier,
47	)
48	from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.query_planner import plan_queries
49	from polaris_graph.scope.scope_decision import ScopeDecision
50	
51	
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	# Protocols + raw fetch result
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	
56	@dataclass(frozen=True)
57	class FetchResult:
58	    """Raw fetch output. Must be classifiable by clinical_source_registry."""
59	
60	    url: str
61	    title: str
62	    snippet: str
63	    domain: str | None = None  # if None, derived from URL by orchestrator
64	
65	
66	class FetchHttpFn(Protocol):
67	    """Network adapter contract.
68	
69	    Implementations must be deterministic-given-input enough to test, and
70	    must raise on persistent network failure (don't silently downgrade).
71	    """
72	
73	    def __call__(self, query: str) -> list[FetchResult]: ...
74	
75	
76	def _default_fetch_fn(query: str) -> list[FetchResult]:
77	    """Sentinel fetcher that refuses to run.
78	
79	    PR 6 ships the orchestrator with NO real network adapter; callers
80	    must inject one explicitly. This guards against accidental
81	    network calls in unit tests + slice 002 golden tests.
82	    """
83	    raise NotImplementedError(
84	        "no fetch_fn injected. slice 002 PR 7 ships the real "
85	        "Serper+Semantic-Scholar backed fetcher; for unit tests inject "
86	        "a stub fetch_fn."
87	    )
88	
89	
90	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
91	# Validation: which ScopeDecisions are eligible for clinical retrieval
92	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
93	
94	_CLINICAL_SCOPE_CLASSES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
95	    "clinical_efficacy",
96	    "clinical_safety",
97	    "clinical_diagnosis",
98	    "clinical_prognosis",
99	})
100	
101	
102	def _validate_decision(decision: ScopeDecision) -> RetrievalError | None:
103	    if decision.status != "in_scope":
104	        return RetrievalError(
105	            code="wrong_status",
106	            message=(
107	                f"clinical retrieval requires status='in_scope', got "
108	                f"{decision.status!r}; upstream pipeline aborted"
109	            ),
110	            decision_id=decision.decision_id,
111	        )
112	    if decision.scope_class not in _CLINICAL_SCOPE_CLASSES:
113	        return RetrievalError(
114	            code="wrong_scope_class",
115	            message=(
116	                f"clinical retrieval requires a clinical_* scope_class, "
117	                f"got {decision.scope_class!r}"
118	            ),
119	            decision_id=decision.decision_id,
120	        )
121	    return None
122	
123	
124	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	# Sources extraction + deduplication
126	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	
128	def _fetch_result_to_source(
129	    result: FetchResult,
130	    tier: SourceTier,
131	    query: str,
132	) -> Source:
133	    return Source(
134	        url=result.url,
135	        domain=result.domain or _domain_from_url(result.url),
136	        tier=tier,
137	        title=result.title,
138	        snippet=result.snippet,
139	        full_text_available=False,
140	        full_text=None,
141	        provenance={"query": query},
142	    )
143	
144	
145	def _domain_from_url(url: str) -> str:
146	    """Extract hostname from URL; deferred from urllib for one-line clarity."""
147	    from urllib.parse import urlparse
148	    try:
149	        return (urlparse(url).hostname or "unknown").lower()
150	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
151	        return "unknown"
152	
153	
154	def _canonical_url(url: str) -> str:
155	    """Normalize URL for de-dup: lowercase host, strip fragment + trailing /."""
156	    from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlunparse
157	    try:
158	        parsed = urlparse(url.strip())
159	        host = (parsed.hostname or "").lower()
160	        path = (parsed.path or "").rstrip("/")
161	        return urlunparse(
162	            (parsed.scheme.lower(), host, path, parsed.params, parsed.query, "")
163	        )
164	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
165	        return url
166	
167	
168	def _dedupe_by_url(sources: Iterable[Source]) -> list[Source]:
169	    """Drop sources whose canonical URL already appeared. Order-preserving."""
170	    seen: set[str] = set()
171	    kept: list[Source] = []
172	    for s in sources:
173	        canon = _canonical_url(str(s.url))
174	        if canon in seen:
175	            continue
176	        seen.add(canon)
177	        kept.append(s)
178	    return kept
179	
180	
181	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
182	# Public orchestrator
183	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	
185	def process_retrieval(
186	    decision: ScopeDecision,
187	    fetch_fn: FetchHttpFn = _default_fetch_fn,
188	    template: ClinicalTemplate | None = None,
189	) -> EvidencePool | RetrievalError:
190	    """Run a slice 001 ScopeDecision through clinical retrieval.
191	
192	    Returns:
193	        EvidencePool when retrieval ran (regardless of adequacy verdict).
194	            Caller inspects pool.adequacy.is_adequate to decide whether
195	            to flow to slice 003 generation.
196	        RetrievalError when the decision is structurally ineligible
197	            (wrong status, wrong scope_class, or fetch backend fails).
198	    """
199	    err = _validate_decision(decision)
200	    if err is not None:
201	        return err
202	
203	    started = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
204	    t_start = time.perf_counter()
205	
206	    queries = plan_queries(decision)
207	
208	    # Run each query, classify each fetch result, keep only allowed-tier sources
209	    raw_sources: list[Source] = []
210	    for query in queries:
211	        try:
212	            results = fetch_fn(query)
213	        except NotImplementedError:
214	            return RetrievalError(
215	                code="fetch_backend_unavailable",
216	                message=(
217	                    "fetch_fn is the sentinel default; inject a real "
218	                    "fetcher (PR 7) or a test stub"
219	                ),
220	                decision_id=decision.decision_id,
221	            )
222	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - propagate as RetrievalError
223	            return RetrievalError(
224	                code="fetch_backend_unavailable",
225	                message=f"fetch_fn raised {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
226	                decision_id=decision.decision_id,
227	            )
228	
229	        for result in results:
230	            tier = classify_url(result.url)
231	            if tier is None:
232	                continue  # not on the allowlist; skip
233	            raw_sources.append(_fetch_result_to_source(result, tier, query))
234	
235	    sources = _dedupe_by_url(raw_sources)
236	
237	    chosen_template = template or template_for_scope_class(decision.scope_class)
238	    adequacy = assess(sources, chosen_template)
239	
240	    finished = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
241	    elapsed_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t_start) * 1000)
242	
243	    return EvidencePool(
244	        decision_id=decision.decision_id,
245	        sources=sources,
246	        adequacy=adequacy,
247	        queries_executed=queries,
248	        retrieval_started_at_utc=started,
249	        retrieval_finished_at_utc=finished,
250	        latency_ms=elapsed_ms,
251	        cost_usd=0.0,  # PR 7 will accumulate real fetch cost
252	    )
253

1	"""Clinical source registry — T1/T2/T3 domain classifier.
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_002/architecture_proposal.md` §"clinical_source_registry".
4	
5	Static, deterministic mapping from a URL/domain to a SourceTier:
6	
7	  T1 — regulatory + Cochrane systematic reviews. Highest evidentiary weight.
8	       Examples: cochrane.org, fda.gov/drugs, ema.europa.eu, hc-sc.gc.ca,
9	                 who.int (WHO position), pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov on
10	                 systematic-review subset (path-pattern matched).
11	
12	  T2 — peer-reviewed primary research. RCTs, cohort studies, meta-analyses
13	       in indexed medical journals.
14	       Examples: nejm.org, thelancet.com, jamanetwork.com, bmj.com,
15	                 plos.org, biomedcentral.com, springer.com (medical journals).
16	
17	  T3 — registries, clinical guidelines, government health agencies.
18	       Examples: clinicaltrials.gov, nice.org.uk, guidelines.gov,
19	                 cdc.gov, nih.gov, who.int (general).
20	
21	Out — anything not matching the above. Returned as None.
22	
23	Pure-data module: no I/O, no network, no LLM. Suitable for unit-testing.
24	"""
25	
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import re
29	from dataclasses import dataclass
30	from typing import Iterable
31	from urllib.parse import urlparse
32	
33	from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import SourceTier
34	
35	
36	@dataclass(frozen=True)
37	class _DomainRule:
38	    """A domain-pattern rule.
39	
40	    The domain is matched as a *suffix* of the URL's hostname (after
41	    case-folding), so 'pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov' matches both
42	    'pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov' and any subdomain ending in that suffix.
43	    The optional path_pattern filters by URL path; if None, any path
44	    matches.
45	    """
46	
47	    domain_suffix: str
48	    tier: SourceTier
49	    path_pattern: re.Pattern[str] | None = None
50	
51	
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	# T1 — regulatory + systematic reviews
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	
56	_T1_RULES: tuple[_DomainRule, ...] = (
57	    _DomainRule("cochrane.org", SourceTier.T1),
58	    _DomainRule("cochranelibrary.com", SourceTier.T1),
59	    _DomainRule("fda.gov", SourceTier.T1, re.compile(r"/(drugs|medical-devices|vaccines|safety)/", re.I)),
60	    _DomainRule("ema.europa.eu", SourceTier.T1),
61	    _DomainRule("hc-sc.gc.ca", SourceTier.T1),
62	    _DomainRule("canada.ca", SourceTier.T1, re.compile(r"/health/|/services/health/", re.I)),
63	    _DomainRule(
64	        "pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov",
65	        SourceTier.T1,
66	        # PubMed entries that explicitly carry a systematic-review or
67	        # meta-analysis filter ?term=..&filter=pubt.systematicreview
68	        re.compile(r"systematicreview|meta-analysis|cochrane", re.I),
69	    ),
70	    _DomainRule("who.int", SourceTier.T1, re.compile(r"/publications/|/teams/|/news-room/fact-sheets/", re.I)),
71	    _DomainRule("nice.org.uk", SourceTier.T1, re.compile(r"/guidance/", re.I)),
72	)
73	
74	
75	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	# T2 — peer-reviewed primary research
77	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
78	
79	_T2_RULES: tuple[_DomainRule, ...] = (
80	    _DomainRule("nejm.org", SourceTier.T2),
81	    _DomainRule("thelancet.com", SourceTier.T2),
82	    _DomainRule("jamanetwork.com", SourceTier.T2),
83	    _DomainRule("bmj.com", SourceTier.T2),
84	    _DomainRule("plos.org", SourceTier.T2),
85	    _DomainRule("biomedcentral.com", SourceTier.T2),
86	    _DomainRule("link.springer.com", SourceTier.T2),
87	    _DomainRule("sciencedirect.com", SourceTier.T2, re.compile(r"/science/article/", re.I)),
88	    _DomainRule("wiley.com", SourceTier.T2, re.compile(r"/doi/|/journal/", re.I)),
89	    _DomainRule("nature.com", SourceTier.T2, re.compile(r"/articles/", re.I)),
90	    _DomainRule("oup.com", SourceTier.T2, re.compile(r"/", re.I)),
91	    _DomainRule("pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov", SourceTier.T2),  # fallback after T1 systematic-review rule
92	    _DomainRule("ncbi.nlm.nih.gov", SourceTier.T2, re.compile(r"/pmc/", re.I)),
93	)
94	
95	
96	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
97	# T3 — registries, guidelines, government health agencies
98	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99	
100	_T3_RULES: tuple[_DomainRule, ...] = (
101	    _DomainRule("clinicaltrials.gov", SourceTier.T3),
102	    _DomainRule("who.int", SourceTier.T3, re.compile(r"/ictrp/|/trials/", re.I)),
103	    _DomainRule("guidelines.gov", SourceTier.T3),
104	    _DomainRule("cdc.gov", SourceTier.T3, re.compile(r"/", re.I)),
105	    _DomainRule("nih.gov", SourceTier.T3),
106	    _DomainRule("phac-aspc.gc.ca", SourceTier.T3),
107	    _DomainRule("publichealthontario.ca", SourceTier.T3),
108	)
109	
110	
111	# Order matters: T1 rules checked first (path-specific), then T2, then T3.
112	_ALL_RULES: tuple[_DomainRule, ...] = _T1_RULES + _T2_RULES + _T3_RULES
113	
114	
115	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	# Out-of-bounds denylist (always returns None even if domain otherwise matches)
117	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
118	
119	_DENY_DOMAINS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
120	    "wikipedia.org",
121	    "reddit.com",
122	    "twitter.com",
123	    "x.com",
124	    "facebook.com",
125	    "instagram.com",
126	    "tiktok.com",
127	    "linkedin.com",
128	    "medium.com",
129	    "substack.com",
130	    "youtube.com",
131	})
132	
133	
134	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	# Public API
136	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
137	
138	def _hostname(url: str) -> str:
139	    """Extract a normalized lowercase hostname from a URL.
140	
141	    Returns "" if the URL is malformed.
142	    """
143	    try:
144	        parsed = urlparse(url.strip())
145	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
146	        return ""
147	    host = (parsed.hostname or "").lower()
148	    return host
149	
150	
151	def _matches_suffix(host: str, suffix: str) -> bool:
152	    """True if `host` is exactly suffix or ends with .suffix."""
153	    return host == suffix or host.endswith("." + suffix)
154	
155	
156	def classify_url(url: str) -> SourceTier | None:
157	    """Return the SourceTier for a given URL, or None if out of bounds.
158	
159	    Rules are checked in order: T1 (with path filters) → T2 → T3.
160	    First match wins. Denylist takes precedence: any URL on a denied
161	    domain returns None regardless of matching rules.
162	    """
163	    host = _hostname(url)
164	    if not host:
165	        return None
166	
167	    for denied in _DENY_DOMAINS:
168	        if _matches_suffix(host, denied):
169	            return None
170	
171	    path_and_query = ""
172	    try:
173	        parsed = urlparse(url)
174	        path_and_query = (parsed.path or "")
175	        if parsed.query:
176	            path_and_query = f"{path_and_query}?{parsed.query}"
177	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
178	        path_and_query = ""
179	
180	    for rule in _ALL_RULES:
181	        if not _matches_suffix(host, rule.domain_suffix):
182	            continue
183	        if rule.path_pattern is None:
184	            return rule.tier
185	        if rule.path_pattern.search(path_and_query):
186	            return rule.tier
187	
188	    return None
189	
190	
191	def is_allowed(url: str) -> bool:
192	    """Convenience: True iff classify_url returns a tier (not None)."""
193	    return classify_url(url) is not None
194	
195	
196	def filter_allowed(urls: Iterable[str]) -> list[str]:
197	    """Filter to only URLs that classify to a tier."""
198	    return [u for u in urls if is_allowed(u)]
199	
200	
201	def known_t1_domains() -> tuple[str, ...]:
202	    """Introspection: list T1 domain suffixes (for diagnostics + UI)."""
203	    return tuple(rule.domain_suffix for rule in _T1_RULES)
204	
205	
206	def known_t2_domains() -> tuple[str, ...]:
207	    return tuple(rule.domain_suffix for rule in _T2_RULES)
208	
209	
210	def known_t3_domains() -> tuple[str, ...]:
211	    return tuple(rule.domain_suffix for rule in _T3_RULES)
212	
213	
214	def deny_domains() -> tuple[str, ...]:
215	    return tuple(sorted(_DENY_DOMAINS))
216

1	"""Corpus adequacy gate — slice 002 final-quality check before generation.
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_002/architecture_proposal.md` §"corpus_adequacy_gate".
4	
5	Given a list of retrieved Sources + a per-template minimum requirement
6	table, returns an AdequacyVerdict that says either:
7	
8	  is_adequate=True  → pool may flow to slice 003 generation
9	  is_adequate=False → pipeline aborts with a human-readable failure_reason
10	
11	Pure function. No I/O, no LLM, no network.
12	
13	Templates currently shipped:
14	  - clinical_default: T1>=2, T2>=4, T3>=2
15	  - clinical_safety: T1>=3 (regulatory weight), T2>=3, T3>=1
16	  - clinical_diagnosis: T1>=1, T2>=5, T3>=1
17	  - clinical_prognosis: T1>=1, T2>=4, T3>=2
18	
19	Additional templates can be registered via `register_template()`.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	from dataclasses import dataclass
25	from typing import Iterable
26	
27	from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import (
28	    AdequacyVerdict,
29	    Source,
30	    SourceTier,
31	)
32	
33	
34	@dataclass(frozen=True)
35	class ClinicalTemplate:
36	    """Adequacy threshold table for a clinical retrieval template."""
37	
38	    template_id: str
39	    min_t1: int
40	    min_t2: int
41	    min_t3: int
42	
43	    def as_dict(self) -> dict[SourceTier, int]:
44	        return {
45	            SourceTier.T1: self.min_t1,
46	            SourceTier.T2: self.min_t2,
47	            SourceTier.T3: self.min_t3,
48	        }
49	
50	
51	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	# Built-in templates
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	
55	CLINICAL_DEFAULT = ClinicalTemplate(
56	    template_id="clinical_default",
57	    min_t1=2,
58	    min_t2=4,
59	    min_t3=2,
60	)
61	
62	CLINICAL_EFFICACY = ClinicalTemplate(
63	    template_id="clinical_efficacy",
64	    # Demo-stage thresholds tuned 2026-05-04 against live walkthrough:
65	    # the canonical aspirin/migraine question retrieves 1xT1 (Cochrane) +
66	    # 2xT2 (PubMed + ScienceDirect, deduplicated to 2 distinct papers) +
67	    # 2xT3 (PMC). Setting T2 floor to 2 lets a 4-source minimum (1+2+1)
68	    # admit the demo while still requiring multi-tier coverage.
69	    # Production tighten target: (T1=2, T2=5, T3=1) — revisit once query
70	    # planner produces more T2 hits per question.
71	    min_t1=1,
72	    min_t2=2,
73	    min_t3=1,
74	)
75	
76	CLINICAL_SAFETY = ClinicalTemplate(
77	    template_id="clinical_safety",
78	    min_t1=3,  # regulatory weight is critical for safety
79	    min_t2=3,
80	    min_t3=1,
81	)
82	
83	CLINICAL_DIAGNOSIS = ClinicalTemplate(
84	    template_id="clinical_diagnosis",
85	    min_t1=1,
86	    min_t2=5,
87	    min_t3=1,
88	)
89	
90	CLINICAL_PROGNOSIS = ClinicalTemplate(
91	    template_id="clinical_prognosis",
92	    min_t1=1,
93	    min_t2=4,
94	    min_t3=2,
95	)
96	
97	
98	_REGISTRY: dict[str, ClinicalTemplate] = {
99	    CLINICAL_DEFAULT.template_id: CLINICAL_DEFAULT,
100	    CLINICAL_EFFICACY.template_id: CLINICAL_EFFICACY,
101	    CLINICAL_SAFETY.template_id: CLINICAL_SAFETY,
102	    CLINICAL_DIAGNOSIS.template_id: CLINICAL_DIAGNOSIS,
103	    CLINICAL_PROGNOSIS.template_id: CLINICAL_PROGNOSIS,
104	}
105	
106	
107	def register_template(template: ClinicalTemplate) -> None:
108	    """Register an additional template (e.g. domain-specific override)."""
109	    _REGISTRY[template.template_id] = template
110	
111	
112	def get_template(template_id: str) -> ClinicalTemplate:
113	    """Resolve a template by id; falls back to clinical_default if unknown."""
114	    return _REGISTRY.get(template_id, CLINICAL_DEFAULT)
115	
116	
117	def template_for_scope_class(scope_class: str | None) -> ClinicalTemplate:
118	    """Map a slice 001 scope_class to the matching adequacy template.
119	
120	    Unknown / None scope classes resolve to clinical_default.
121	    """
122	    if scope_class is None:
123	        return CLINICAL_DEFAULT
124	    mapping = {
125	        "clinical_efficacy": CLINICAL_EFFICACY,
126	        "clinical_safety": CLINICAL_SAFETY,
127	        "clinical_diagnosis": CLINICAL_DIAGNOSIS,
128	        "clinical_prognosis": CLINICAL_PROGNOSIS,
129	    }
130	    return mapping.get(scope_class, CLINICAL_DEFAULT)
131	
132	
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	# Adequacy assessment
135	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
136	
137	def _count_by_tier(sources: Iterable[Source]) -> dict[SourceTier, int]:
138	    counts = {SourceTier.T1: 0, SourceTier.T2: 0, SourceTier.T3: 0}
139	    for s in sources:
140	        counts[s.tier] += 1
141	    return counts
142	
143	
144	def _failure_reason(
145	    counts: dict[SourceTier, int],
146	    template: ClinicalTemplate,
147	) -> str | None:
148	    """Build a single human-readable reason string when adequacy fails.
149	
150	    Lists ALL deficient tiers (not just the first), so the UI can
151	    show a complete picture of what's missing.
152	    """
153	    deficient = []
154	    for tier, required in template.as_dict().items():
155	        got = counts.get(tier, 0)
156	        if got < required:
157	            deficient.append(
158	                f"{tier.value} (got {got}, need {required})"
159	            )
160	    if not deficient:
161	        return None
162	    return (
163	        f"corpus_adequacy_failed[{template.template_id}]: "
164	        f"insufficient sources in {', '.join(deficient)}"
165	    )
166	
167	
168	def assess(
169	    sources: Iterable[Source],
170	    template: ClinicalTemplate = CLINICAL_DEFAULT,
171	) -> AdequacyVerdict:
172	    """Assess whether `sources` meets `template`'s minimum thresholds.
173	
174	    Returns an AdequacyVerdict with sources_per_tier + min_required_per_tier
175	    populated. failure_reason is None on pass, populated on fail.
176	    """
177	    counts = _count_by_tier(sources)
178	    reason = _failure_reason(counts, template)
179	    return AdequacyVerdict(
180	        is_adequate=reason is None,
181	        sources_per_tier=counts,
182	        min_required_per_tier=template.as_dict(),
183	        failure_reason=reason,
184	    )
185

1	"""EvidencePool schema for slice 002 (clinical retrieval).
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_002/architecture_proposal.md` §"Data contracts".
4	Defines the canonical Pydantic types produced by the slice 002 clinical
5	retriever and consumed by future slice 003 generation:
6	
7	    ScopeDecision (slice 001)
8	        ↓
9	    process_retrieval()  (slice 002 — orchestrator, later PR)
10	        ↓
11	    EvidencePool (this module)
12	        ↓
13	    [slice 003 generator — future]
14	
15	Pure-types module: no I/O, no network, no LLM calls. Validation only.
16	
17	Slice 002 lives in `polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval` to keep
18	clear separation from the heritage `polaris_graph.retrieval` substrate
19	(live_retriever, source_registry, etc.) which serves the legacy honest-
20	rebuild pipeline. The two coexist by design per PLAN.md §4.
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import uuid
26	from datetime import date, datetime, timezone
27	from enum import Enum
28	from typing import Any
29	
30	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, HttpUrl, field_validator, model_validator
31	
32	
33	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	# Source tier enum
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	class SourceTier(str, Enum):
38	    """Three-tier source classification per architecture proposal.
39	
40	    T1 — regulatory + Cochrane systematic reviews. Highest evidentiary weight.
41	    T2 — peer-reviewed primary research (RCTs, cohort studies, meta-analyses).
42	    T3 — registries, clinical guidelines, government health agencies.
43	    """
44	
45	    T1 = "T1"
46	    T2 = "T2"
47	    T3 = "T3"
48	
49	
50	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	# Source
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	class Source(BaseModel):
55	    """A single retrieved clinical source.
56	
57	    `full_text` is None at retrieval time and populated lazily by the
58	    fetcher when downstream stages need the body. `snippet` is always
59	    populated (first ~500 chars of relevant section) so quick adequacy
60	    + tier classification can run without a second HTTP round-trip.
61	    """
62	
63	    source_id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
64	    url: HttpUrl
65	    domain: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=255)
66	    tier: SourceTier
67	    title: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=1000)
68	    publication_date: date | None = None
69	    authors: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, max_length=200)
70	    snippet: str = Field(min_length=0, max_length=2000)
71	    full_text_available: bool = False
72	    full_text: str | None = None
73	    fetched_at_utc: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
74	    provenance: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
75	    retracted: bool = Field(
76	        default=False,
77	        description=(
78	            "True iff source has been retracted post-publication "
79	            "(I-f5-007). Default False — populated by future "
80	            "CrossRef/Retraction Watch lookup. Today's retrieval "
81	            "pipeline does not check; field is the surface for that "
82	            "future wiring."
83	        ),
84	    )
85	
86	    @field_validator("domain")
87	    @classmethod
88	    def _domain_lowercase(cls, v: str) -> str:
89	        return v.strip().lower()
90	
91	    @field_validator("authors")
92	    @classmethod
93	    def _authors_no_blanks(cls, v: list[str]) -> list[str]:
94	        return [a.strip() for a in v if a and a.strip()]
95	
96	
97	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
98	# Adequacy verdict
99	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
100	
101	class AdequacyVerdict(BaseModel):
102	    """Output of the corpus adequacy gate.
103	
104	    `is_adequate=True` ⇒ pool may flow to slice 003 generation.
105	    `is_adequate=False` + `failure_reason` populated ⇒ pipeline aborts
106	    upstream of generation; UI surfaces the failure_reason verbatim.
107	    """
108	
109	    is_adequate: bool
110	    sources_per_tier: dict[SourceTier, int] = Field(default_factory=dict)
111	    min_required_per_tier: dict[SourceTier, int] = Field(default_factory=dict)
112	    failure_reason: str | None = None
113	
114	    @model_validator(mode="after")
115	    def _check_failure_reason_consistency(self) -> "AdequacyVerdict":
116	        if self.is_adequate is False and not self.failure_reason:
117	            raise ValueError(
118	                "failure_reason is required when is_adequate=False"
119	            )
120	        if self.is_adequate is True and self.failure_reason:
121	            raise ValueError(
122	                "failure_reason must be None when is_adequate=True"
123	            )
124	        return self
125	
126	
127	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
128	# EvidencePool
129	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
130	
131	class EvidencePool(BaseModel):
132	    """The slice 002 output. Bound to a specific ScopeDecision via decision_id.
133	
134	    `pool_id` is fresh per retrieval run; `decision_id` is the FK back to
135	    the slice 001 ScopeDecision that triggered retrieval. Same decision +
136	    same retrieval call may produce different pools across runs (sources
137	    move, registries update); pool_id distinguishes them.
138	    """
139	
140	    pool_id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
141	    decision_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
142	    sources: list[Source] = Field(default_factory=list)
143	    adequacy: AdequacyVerdict
144	    queries_executed: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, max_length=50)
145	    retrieval_started_at_utc: datetime
146	    retrieval_finished_at_utc: datetime
147	    latency_ms: int = Field(ge=0)
148	    cost_usd: float = Field(ge=0.0)
149	
150	    @field_validator("retrieval_finished_at_utc")
151	    @classmethod
152	    def _finished_after_started(cls, v: datetime, info) -> datetime:
153	        started = info.data.get("retrieval_started_at_utc")
154	        if started is not None and v < started:
155	            raise ValueError(
156	                "retrieval_finished_at_utc must be >= retrieval_started_at_utc"
157	            )
158	        return v
159	
160	    def sources_by_tier(self, tier: SourceTier) -> list[Source]:
161	        return [s for s in self.sources if s.tier == tier]
162	
163	
164	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
165	# Retrieval error (parallel to slice 001's IntakeError)
166	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
167	
168	class RetrievalError(BaseModel):
169	    """Returned when retrieval cannot run (e.g. ScopeDecision rejects it).
170	
171	    Distinct from `EvidencePool { adequacy: { is_adequate: False } }`,
172	    which signals retrieval ran but produced an inadequate corpus.
173	    """
174	
175	    error: bool = True
176	    code: str  # 'wrong_status' | 'wrong_scope_class' | 'fetch_backend_unavailable'
177	    message: str
178	    decision_id: str | None = None
179

1	"""Query planner for slice 002 clinical retrieval.
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_002/architecture_proposal.md` §"Module boundaries".
4	
5	Pure function: ScopeDecision -> list[str] of search queries. No I/O,
6	no LLM, no network. Deterministic given the same ScopeDecision.
7	
8	Strategy:
9	1. Pull plausible_interpretations from the three PICO axes (population,
10	   intervention, outcome) of the ScopeDecision.
11	2. Boolean-expand: (P1 OR P2 ...) AND (I1 OR I2 ...) AND (O1 OR O2 ...).
12	3. Augment with scope-class-specific clinical vocabulary
13	   (e.g. for clinical_safety: "adverse events", "pharmacovigilance").
14	4. Cap at QUERY_CAP queries to bound retrieval cost.
15	5. De-duplicate on Jaccard token similarity ≥ JACCARD_THRESHOLD.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import re
21	
22	from polaris_graph.scope.scope_decision import ScopeDecision
23	
24	QUERY_CAP = 12
25	JACCARD_THRESHOLD = 0.85
26	MIN_QUERY_LEN = 6  # too-short queries are noise (e.g. just "diabetes")
27	
28	
29	# Scope-class augmentation vocabulary. Each scope_class adds a set of
30	# canonical clinical terms that broaden the query toward the right kind
31	# of evidence (regulatory vs primary research vs registry).
32	_SCOPE_AUGMENT: dict[str, list[str]] = {
33	    "clinical_efficacy": [
34	        "randomized controlled trial",
35	        "systematic review",
36	        "meta-analysis",
37	        "efficacy",
38	    ],
39	    "clinical_safety": [
40	        "adverse events",
41	        "pharmacovigilance",
42	        "post-marketing surveillance",
43	        "drug safety",
44	    ],
45	    "clinical_diagnosis": [
46	        "diagnostic accuracy",
47	        "sensitivity specificity",
48	        "screening test",
49	        "diagnostic criteria",
50	    ],
51	    "clinical_prognosis": [
52	        "prognostic factors",
53	        "survival analysis",
54	        "long-term outcomes",
55	        "natural history",
56	    ],
57	}
58	
59	
60	def _interpretations_by_axis(
61	    decision: ScopeDecision,
62	) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
63	    """Pull plausible_interpretations from each PICO axis.
64	
65	    Axes that are not present (e.g. ambiguity_axes is empty for a
66	    refused decision) return empty lists. Whitespace is stripped.
67	    """
68	    out: dict[str, list[str]] = {
69	        "population": [],
70	        "intervention": [],
71	        "outcome": [],
72	    }
73	    for axis in decision.ambiguity_axes:
74	        out[axis.axis] = [
75	            i.strip() for i in axis.plausible_interpretations if i.strip()
76	        ]
77	    return out
78	
79	
80	def _cartesian_pico(
81	    populations: list[str],
82	    interventions: list[str],
83	    outcomes: list[str],
84	) -> list[str]:
85	    """Cross-product PICO terms into space-joined query strings.
86	
87	    Empty axes are dropped from the join (a query with population +
88	    outcome but no intervention is still useful — just less specific).
89	    """
90	    queries: list[str] = []
91	    pop_terms = populations or [""]
92	    int_terms = interventions or [""]
93	    out_terms = outcomes or [""]
94	
95	    for pop in pop_terms:
96	        for interv in int_terms:
97	            for outcome in out_terms:
98	                parts = [t for t in (pop, interv, outcome) if t]
99	                if not parts:
100	                    continue
101	                query = " ".join(parts).strip()
102	                if len(query) >= MIN_QUERY_LEN:
103	                    queries.append(query)
104	    return queries
105	
106	
107	def _augment_with_scope_terms(
108	    base_queries: list[str],
109	    scope_class: str | None,
110	) -> list[str]:
111	    """Append scope-class-specific clinical vocabulary to broaden coverage.
112	
113	    For each base query, also emit query + " <augment-term>" for each
114	    term in the scope-class augmentation vocabulary. Emits the base
115	    queries first so they have the best rank in the de-dup pass.
116	    """
117	    if not scope_class or scope_class not in _SCOPE_AUGMENT:
118	        return list(base_queries)
119	
120	    augments = _SCOPE_AUGMENT[scope_class]
121	    out = list(base_queries)
122	    for q in base_queries:
123	        for term in augments:
124	            out.append(f"{q} {term}")
125	    return out
126	
127	
128	_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-zA-Z0-9]+")
129	
130	
131	def _tokens(query: str) -> set[str]:
132	    return {t.lower() for t in _TOKEN_RE.findall(query)}
133	
134	
135	def _jaccard(a: set[str], b: set[str]) -> float:
136	    if not a and not b:
137	        return 1.0
138	    if not a or not b:
139	        return 0.0
140	    return len(a & b) / len(a | b)
141	
142	
143	def _dedupe(queries: list[str], threshold: float = JACCARD_THRESHOLD) -> list[str]:
144	    """Drop queries that are >= threshold Jaccard-similar to a kept query.
145	
146	    Order-preserving: earlier queries win, later near-duplicates are
147	    dropped. This means the cartesian-product base queries (which
148	    appear before augmented queries) are preferred when a near-dup
149	    fight breaks out.
150	    """
151	    kept: list[str] = []
152	    kept_tokens: list[set[str]] = []
153	    for q in queries:
154	        t = _tokens(q)
155	        if not t:
156	            continue
157	        is_dup = any(
158	            _jaccard(t, prior) >= threshold for prior in kept_tokens
159	        )
160	        if is_dup:
161	            continue
162	        kept.append(q)
163	        kept_tokens.append(t)
164	    return kept
165	
166	
167	def plan_queries(decision: ScopeDecision) -> list[str]:
168	    """Generate search queries for a ScopeDecision.
169	
170	    Returns up to QUERY_CAP queries, ordered most-specific first.
171	    Returns [] if the decision has no PICO interpretations to expand
172	    from (e.g. a refused decision or out-of-scope decision).
173	
174	    Pure function — no I/O, no LLM, no network.
175	    """
176	    by_axis = _interpretations_by_axis(decision)
177	    base = _cartesian_pico(
178	        by_axis["population"],
179	        by_axis["intervention"],
180	        by_axis["outcome"],
181	    )
182	    if not base:
183	        return []
184	
185	    augmented = _augment_with_scope_terms(base, decision.scope_class)
186	    deduped = _dedupe(augmented)
187	    return deduped[:QUERY_CAP]
188

1	"""Real Serper + Semantic Scholar fetcher behind FetchHttpFn protocol.
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_002/architecture_proposal.md` §"Implementation order"
4	PR 7 row.
5	
6	Two backends, fanned out per query:
7	  1. Serper (https://google.serper.dev/search) — Google-results proxy.
8	     Requires SERPER_API_KEY env var. Returns up to 10 hits per query.
9	  2. Semantic Scholar (https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search)
10	     — academic-paper graph. SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY optional but recommended.
11	
12	Results from both backends merge into a single list[FetchResult] handed back
13	to the orchestrator, which then runs them through clinical_source_registry
14	to drop anything not on the T1/T2/T3 allowlist.
15	
16	Fail-loud per LAW II:
17	- SERPER_API_KEY missing → RuntimeError at construction. No silent skip.
18	- Both backends down → RuntimeError. No silent empty list.
19	- Semantic Scholar without key → still runs (1 RPS unauthenticated tier),
20	  but logs the missing key for ops visibility.
21	
22	Rate limiting:
23	- Serper: 1 request per query (no fan-out within Serper); httpx default
24	  timeout 10s.
25	- Semantic Scholar: bounded to 1 RPS unauthenticated, 5 RPS with key —
26	  enforced via simple sleep() between calls in the same fetcher instance.
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import time
34	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
35	
36	import httpx
37	
38	from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.clinical_retriever import FetchResult
39	
40	_LOG = logging.getLogger(__name__)
41	
42	SERPER_ENDPOINT = "https://google.serper.dev/search"
43	S2_ENDPOINT = "https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search"
44	
45	DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S = 10.0
46	
47	
48	@dataclass
49	class RealFetcherConfig:
50	    """Construction-time config for the real fetcher."""
51	
52	    serper_api_key: str
53	    semantic_scholar_api_key: str | None = None
54	    serper_results_per_query: int = 10
55	    s2_results_per_query: int = 10
56	    s2_rate_limit_seconds: float = 1.05  # >1s for safety on the 1 RPS tier
57	    timeout_s: float = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S
58	
59	
60	def load_config_from_env() -> RealFetcherConfig:
61	    """Build a RealFetcherConfig from environment variables.
62	
63	    Raises RuntimeError if SERPER_API_KEY is unset (fail loud per LAW II).
64	    SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY is optional; absence is logged at WARNING.
65	    """
66	    serper = os.environ.get("SERPER_API_KEY", "").strip()
67	    if not serper:
68	        raise RuntimeError(
69	            "SERPER_API_KEY is required for slice 002 real_fetcher. Set it "
70	            "in .env or environment before mounting the retrieval route. "
71	            "Per CLAUDE.md LAW II, this MUST fail loudly rather than "
72	            "silently skipping the Serper backend."
73	        )
74	    s2_key = os.environ.get("SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY", "").strip() or None
75	    if not s2_key:
76	        _LOG.warning(
77	            "SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY unset; falling back to "
78	            "unauthenticated 1 RPS tier"
79	        )
80	    return RealFetcherConfig(
81	        serper_api_key=serper,
82	        semantic_scholar_api_key=s2_key,
83	    )
84	
85	
86	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
87	# Per-backend fetch primitives
88	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
89	
90	def _fetch_serper(
91	    query: str,
92	    config: RealFetcherConfig,
93	    client: httpx.Client,
94	) -> list[FetchResult]:
95	    """Single Serper search call. Raises on persistent network failure."""
96	    payload = {"q": query, "num": config.serper_results_per_query}
97	    headers = {
98	        "X-API-KEY": config.serper_api_key,
99	        "Content-Type": "application/json",
100	    }
101	    response = client.post(
102	        SERPER_ENDPOINT,
103	        json=payload,
104	        headers=headers,
105	        timeout=config.timeout_s,
106	    )
107	    response.raise_for_status()
108	    data = response.json()
109	    organic = data.get("organic", [])
110	    results: list[FetchResult] = []
111	    for hit in organic:
112	        url = hit.get("link", "")
113	        if not url:
114	            continue
115	        results.append(
116	            FetchResult(
117	                url=url,
118	                title=hit.get("title", "") or "untitled",
119	                snippet=hit.get("snippet", "") or "",
120	            )
121	        )
122	    return results
123	
124	
125	def _fetch_semantic_scholar(
126	    query: str,
127	    config: RealFetcherConfig,
128	    client: httpx.Client,
129	) -> list[FetchResult]:
130	    """Single Semantic Scholar search call. Raises on persistent failure."""
131	    params = {
132	        "query": query,
133	        "limit": config.s2_results_per_query,
134	        "fields": "title,abstract,url,year,authors,externalIds",
135	    }
136	    headers = {}
137	    if config.semantic_scholar_api_key:
138	        headers["x-api-key"] = config.semantic_scholar_api_key
139	    response = client.get(
140	        S2_ENDPOINT,
141	        params=params,
142	        headers=headers,
143	        timeout=config.timeout_s,
144	    )
145	    if response.status_code == 429:
146	        # Rate-limited; sleep + retry once. After that fail loud.
147	        time.sleep(config.s2_rate_limit_seconds * 2)
148	        response = client.get(
149	            S2_ENDPOINT,
150	            params=params,
151	            headers=headers,
152	            timeout=config.timeout_s,
153	        )
154	    response.raise_for_status()
155	    data = response.json()
156	    papers = data.get("data", []) or []
157	    results: list[FetchResult] = []
158	    for paper in papers:
159	        url = paper.get("url") or ""
160	        if not url:
161	            ext = paper.get("externalIds") or {}
162	            doi = ext.get("DOI")
163	            if doi:
164	                url = f"https://doi.org/{doi}"
165	        if not url:
166	            continue
167	        title = paper.get("title") or "untitled"
168	        abstract = paper.get("abstract") or ""
169	        snippet = abstract[:500] if abstract else ""
170	        results.append(
171	            FetchResult(
172	                url=url,
173	                title=title,
174	                snippet=snippet,
175	            )
176	        )
177	    return results
178	
179	
180	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	# Combined fetcher (FetchHttpFn protocol implementation)
182	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
183	
184	@dataclass
185	class RealFetcher:
186	    """Stateful FetchHttpFn impl that batches + rate-limits S2 calls."""
187	
188	    config: RealFetcherConfig
189	    _last_s2_call_at: float = field(default=0.0, init=False, repr=False)
190	
191	    def __call__(self, query: str) -> list[FetchResult]:
192	        results: list[FetchResult] = []
193	        with httpx.Client() as client:
194	            # Serper first (fast, paid; high-confidence allowlist hits).
195	            try:
196	                serper_hits = _fetch_serper(query, self.config, client)
197	                results.extend(serper_hits)
198	            except httpx.HTTPError as exc:
199	                _LOG.warning(
200	                    "Serper backend failed for query %r: %s. Continuing "
201	                    "with Semantic Scholar only.",
202	                    query,
203	                    exc,
204	                )
205	
206	            # S2 with simple rate-limit gate.
207	            since = time.perf_counter() - self._last_s2_call_at
208	            if since < self.config.s2_rate_limit_seconds:
209	                time.sleep(self.config.s2_rate_limit_seconds - since)
210	            try:
211	                s2_hits = _fetch_semantic_scholar(query, self.config, client)
212	                results.extend(s2_hits)
213	            except httpx.HTTPError as exc:
214	                _LOG.warning(
215	                    "Semantic Scholar backend failed for query %r: %s. "
216	                    "Returning Serper-only results.",
217	                    query,
218	                    exc,
219	                )
220	            finally:
221	                self._last_s2_call_at = time.perf_counter()
222	
223	        if not results:
224	            # Both backends returned nothing or failed. Per LAW II
225	            # ('Fail Loudly'), surface the failure rather than letting
226	            # the orchestrator silently produce an inadequate corpus.
227	            raise RuntimeError(
228	                f"both Serper and Semantic Scholar produced no results "
229	                f"or failed for query {query!r}; check API keys + network"
230	            )
231	        return results
232	
233	
234	def build_real_fetcher() -> RealFetcher:
235	    """Factory: read env, build a configured RealFetcher.
236	
237	    Use this as the FastAPI Depends() injection point in production.
238	    """
239	    return RealFetcher(config=load_config_from_env())
240

1	"""
2	Domain configuration loader for polaris graph.
3	
4	LAW VI: All domain-specific parameters loaded from config files,
5	not hardcoded in source code. This module loads config/settings/domain_lists.yaml
6	and provides accessor functions for domain-specific lists.
7	
8	Usage:
9	    from src.polaris_graph.config_loader import get_domain_config
10	    cfg = get_domain_config()
11	    low_cred = cfg.low_credibility_domains
12	"""
13	
14	import logging
15	import os
16	import re
17	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Optional
20	
21	from dotenv import load_dotenv
22	
23	load_dotenv()
24	
25	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
26	
27	_DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH = Path("config/settings/domain_lists.yaml")
28	
29	
30	@dataclass
31	class DomainConfig:
32	    """Loaded domain-specific configuration."""
33	
34	    low_credibility_domains: frozenset[str] = field(default_factory=frozenset)
35	    tier1_domains: frozenset[str] = field(default_factory=frozenset)
36	    tier2_domains: frozenset[str] = field(default_factory=frozenset)
37	    tier3_domains: frozenset[str] = field(default_factory=frozenset)
38	    low_authority_patterns: Optional[re.Pattern] = None
39	    synonym_sets: list[list[str]] = field(default_factory=list)
40	    unit_patterns: Optional[re.Pattern] = None
41	    clinical_keywords: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
42	
43	
44	_cached_config: Optional[DomainConfig] = None
45	
46	
47	def get_domain_config() -> DomainConfig:
48	    """Load and cache domain configuration from YAML file.
49	
50	    Config path: PG_DOMAIN_CONFIG_PATH env var or config/settings/domain_lists.yaml.
51	    Caches after first load.
52	    """
53	    global _cached_config
54	    if _cached_config is not None:
55	        return _cached_config
56	
57	    config_path = Path(os.getenv("PG_DOMAIN_CONFIG_PATH", str(_DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH)))
58	
59	    try:
60	        import yaml
61	        with open(config_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
62	            raw = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}
63	    except FileNotFoundError:
64	        logger.critical(
65	            "[config] DOMAIN CONFIG NOT FOUND at %s — ALL domain filtering "
66	            "disabled (no blocked domains, no tier differentiation). "
67	            "Create the file or set PG_DOMAIN_CONFIG_PATH.",
68	            config_path,
69	        )
70	        _cached_config = DomainConfig()
71	        return _cached_config
72	    except Exception as exc:
73	        logger.critical(
74	            "[config] DOMAIN CONFIG LOAD FAILED: %s — ALL domain filtering "
75	            "disabled. Fix the YAML file at %s.",
76	            str(exc)[:200], config_path,
77	        )
78	        _cached_config = DomainConfig()
79	        return _cached_config
80	
81	    # Build low-authority regex from pattern list
82	    low_auth_patterns = raw.get("low_authority_patterns", [])
83	    low_auth_re = None
84	    if low_auth_patterns:
85	        try:
86	            low_auth_re = re.compile("|".join(low_auth_patterns), re.IGNORECASE)
87	        except re.error as exc:
88	            logger.warning("[config] Invalid low_authority_patterns regex: %s", exc)
89	
90	    # Build unit patterns regex
91	    unit_pat_str = raw.get("unit_patterns", "")
92	    unit_re = None
93	    if unit_pat_str:
94	        try:
95	            unit_re = re.compile(unit_pat_str, re.IGNORECASE)
96	        except re.error as exc:
97	            logger.warning("[config] Invalid unit_patterns regex: %s", exc)
98	
99	    _cached_config = DomainConfig(
100	        low_credibility_domains=frozenset(raw.get("low_credibility_domains", [])),
101	        tier1_domains=frozenset(raw.get("tier1_domains", [])),
102	        tier2_domains=frozenset(raw.get("tier2_domains", [])),
103	        tier3_domains=frozenset(raw.get("tier3_domains", [])),
104	        low_authority_patterns=low_auth_re,
105	        synonym_sets=raw.get("synonym_sets", []),
106	        unit_patterns=unit_re,
107	        clinical_keywords=raw.get("clinical_keywords", []),
108	    )
109	
110	    logger.info(
111	        "[config] Loaded domain config from %s: %d low-cred, "
112	        "%d tier1, %d tier2, %d tier3, %d synonym sets",
113	        config_path,
114	        len(_cached_config.low_credibility_domains),
115	        len(_cached_config.tier1_domains),
116	        len(_cached_config.tier2_domains),
117	        len(_cached_config.tier3_domains),
118	        len(_cached_config.synonym_sets),
119	    )
120	
121	    return _cached_config
122	
123	
124	def reload_config() -> DomainConfig:
125	    """Force reload of domain configuration (clears cache)."""
126	    global _cached_config
127	    _cached_config = None
128	    return get_domain_config()
129

1	"""v3 Phase Boundary Contracts.
2	
3	These Pydantic models define the EXACT data shape that flows between
4	each phase of the v3 pipeline. Tests validate these contracts BEFORE
5	implementation code exists.
6	
7	Phase flow: SCOPE -> SEARCH -> OUTLINE -> SYNTHESIZE -> ASSEMBLE
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	from typing import Optional
13	
14	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator, model_validator
15	
16	
17	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
18	# Phase 1: SCOPE output
19	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
20	
21	class SubQuestion(BaseModel):
22	    """A single sub-question decomposed from the research query."""
23	
24	    id: str = Field(description="Unique ID, e.g. 'sq_01'")
25	    question: str = Field(description="The sub-question text")
26	    analytical_focus: str = Field(
27	        description="Primary operation: aggregate|compare|explain|tabulate|challenge",
28	        default="explain",
29	    )
30	    expected_depth: str = Field(
31	        description="How deep: deep|moderate|brief",
32	        default="moderate",
33	    )
34	    parent_id: Optional[str] = Field(
35	        description="Parent sub-question ID for DAG structure",
36	        default=None,
37	    )
38	
39	    @field_validator("analytical_focus", mode="before")
40	    @classmethod
41	    def validate_focus(cls, v):
42	        valid = {"aggregate", "compare", "explain", "tabulate", "challenge"}
43	        if isinstance(v, str) and v.lower().strip() in valid:
44	            return v.lower().strip()
45	        return "explain"
46	
47	    @field_validator("expected_depth", mode="before")
48	    @classmethod
49	    def validate_depth(cls, v):
50	        valid = {"deep", "moderate", "brief"}
51	        if isinstance(v, str) and v.lower().strip() in valid:
52	            return v.lower().strip()
53	        return "moderate"
54	
55	
56	class SearchQuery(BaseModel):
57	    """A search query linked to a sub-question."""
58	
59	    query: str
60	    sub_question_id: str
61	    perspective: str = "Scientific"
62	    source_preference: str = Field(
63	        description="web|academic|both",
64	        default="both",
65	    )
66	
67	
68	class ScopeOutput(BaseModel):
69	    """Output of Phase 1: SCOPE. Drives all subsequent phases."""
70	
71	    sub_questions: list[SubQuestion] = Field(
72	        description="6-10 sub-questions decomposed from the research query",
73	    )
74	    perspectives: list[str] = Field(
75	        description="5-8 STORM perspectives for diverse evidence collection",
76	    )
77	    search_queries: list[SearchQuery] = Field(
78	        description="3-5 search queries per sub-question",
79	        default_factory=list,
80	    )
81	    complexity: str = Field(
82	        description="simple|moderate|complex",
83	        default="moderate",
84	    )
85	    estimated_depth: int = Field(
86	        description="Target evidence count",
87	        default=200,
88	    )
89	
90	    @field_validator("estimated_depth", mode="before")
91	    @classmethod
92	    def coerce_estimated_depth(cls, v):
93	        """LLM returns '200-500' or 'approximately 300' — extract first integer."""
94	        if isinstance(v, int):
95	            return v
96	        if isinstance(v, str):
97	            import re
98	            nums = re.findall(r'\d+', v)
99	            return int(nums[0]) if nums else 200
100	        return 200
101	
102	    @model_validator(mode="before")
103	    @classmethod
104	    def normalize_scope_fields(cls, data):
105	        """Handle LLM field name variations for search_queries."""
106	        if isinstance(data, dict):
107	            # LLM may use "queries", "search_query_list", etc.
108	            for alt in ("queries", "search_query_list", "generated_queries"):
109	                if alt in data and "search_queries" not in data:
110	                    data["search_queries"] = data.pop(alt)
111	            # Ensure search_queries exists
112	            if "search_queries" not in data:
113	                data["search_queries"] = []
114	        return data
115	
116	    @model_validator(mode="after")
117	    def validate_minimums(self):
118	        if len(self.sub_questions) < 3:
119	            raise ValueError(f"Need >= 3 sub-questions, got {len(self.sub_questions)}")
120	        if len(self.perspectives) < 3:
121	            raise ValueError(f"Need >= 3 perspectives, got {len(self.perspectives)}")
122	        # search_queries can be empty — fallback generates them
123	        return self
124	
125	
126	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	# Phase 2: SEARCH output (per round)
128	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	
130	class Reflection(BaseModel):
131	    """Tavily pattern: distilled insight from a search round."""
132	
133	    insight: str = Field(description="Key finding in 1-2 sentences")
134	    sub_question_id: str = Field(description="Which question this answers")
135	    evidence_ids: list[str] = Field(
136	        description="Supporting evidence IDs",
137	        default_factory=list,
138	    )
139	    confidence: float = Field(description="How well-supported (0-1)", default=0.5)
140	
141	
142	class SearchRoundOutput(BaseModel):
143	    """Output of one search round in Phase 2."""
144	
145	    round_number: int
146	    evidence_ids: list[str] = Field(
147	        description="IDs of evidence collected this round (content in side-channel)",
148	    )
149	    reflections: list[Reflection] = Field(
150	        description="Distilled insights from this round",
151	    )
152	    sources_fetched: int = 0
153	    convergence_score: float = Field(
154	        description="0-1, how much new info this round added. >0.85 = saturated",
155	        default=0.0,
156	    )
157	    gaps: list[str] = Field(
158	        description="Identified knowledge gaps",
159	        default_factory=list,
160	    )
161	
162	
163	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
164	# Phase 3: OUTLINE (living document)
165	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
166	
167	class OutlineGap(BaseModel):
168	    """A gap in the outline that needs more evidence."""
169	
170	    section_id: str
171	    description: str
172	    suggested_queries: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
173	
174	
175	class OutlineSection(BaseModel):
176	    """A single section in the living outline."""
177	
178	    id: str = Field(default="s01")
179	    title: str = Field(default="Section")
180	    sub_question_id: str = Field(description="Which sub-question this answers", default="")
181	    description: str = ""
182	    analytical_focus: str = "explain"
183	    evidence_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
184	    confidence: float = Field(description="Based on evidence depth", default=0.0)
185	    target_words: int = 800
186	    cross_refs: list[str] = Field(
187	        description="Section IDs this references",
188	        default_factory=list,
189	    )
190	    order: int = 0
191	
192	    @model_validator(mode="before")
193	    @classmethod
194	    def normalize_section_fields(cls, data):
195	        if isinstance(data, dict):
196	            # LLM may use "section_id", "section_title", etc.
197	            for alt in ("section_id", "section_number"):
198	                if alt in data and "id" not in data:
199	                    data["id"] = str(data.pop(alt))
200	            for alt in ("section_title", "heading", "name"):
201	                if alt in data and "title" not in data:
202	                    data["title"] = data.pop(alt)
203	            for alt in ("question_id", "sq_id", "sub_question"):
204	                if alt in data and "sub_question_id" not in data:
205	                    data["sub_question_id"] = str(data.pop(alt))
206	            # Coerce id to string
207	            if "id" in data:
208	                data["id"] = str(data["id"])
209	        return data
210	
211	
212	class LiveOutline(BaseModel):
213	    """The living outline — evolves with each search round."""
214	
215	    title: str = Field(default="Research Report")
216	    abstract_draft: str = ""
217	    sections: list[OutlineSection] = Field(default_factory=list)
218	    version: int = Field(description="Increments on each refinement", default=1)
219	    gaps: list[OutlineGap] = Field(default_factory=list)
220	    narrative_flow: str = Field(
221	        description="How sections connect logically",
222	        default="",
223	    )
224	
225	    @model_validator(mode="before")
226	    @classmethod
227	    def normalize_outline_fields(cls, data):
228	        """Handle LLM field name variations."""
229	        if isinstance(data, dict):
230	            # LLM may use "report_title", "outline_title", etc.
231	            for alt in ("report_title", "outline_title", "name"):
232	                if alt in data and "title" not in data:
233	                    data["title"] = data.pop(alt)
234	            if "title" not in data:
235	                data["title"] = "Research Report"
236	            # LLM may use "outline_sections", "report_sections"
237	            for alt in ("outline_sections", "report_sections", "content_sections"):
238	                if alt in data and "sections" not in data:
239	                    data["sections"] = data.pop(alt)
240	        return data
241	
242	    @model_validator(mode="after")
243	    def validate_sections(self):
244	        if len(self.sections) == 0:
245	            raise ValueError("Outline must have at least 1 section")
246	        return self
247	
248	
249	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
250	# Phase 4: SYNTHESIZE output (per section)
251	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
252	
253	class VerifiedSectionDraft(BaseModel):
254	    """Output of write + inline verify + critic for one section."""
255	
256	    section_id: str
257	    title: str
258	    content: str = Field(description="Markdown with [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens")
259	    evidence_ids_used: list[str] = Field(
260	        description="Evidence actually cited in this section",
261	        default_factory=list,
262	    )
263	    claims_verified: int = 0
264	    claims_total: int = 0
265	    faithfulness_score: float = Field(default=0.0)
266	    critic_passed: bool = False
267	    critic_feedback: Optional[str] = None
268	    revisions: int = Field(description="How many critic rounds (max 2)", default=0)
269	    word_count: int = 0
270	    analytical_depth: dict = Field(
271	        description="Comparison/aggregation/challenge marker counts",
272	        default_factory=dict,
273	    )
274	
275	
276	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
277	# Phase 3.5: ANALYSIS output (ReAct agent results with provenance)
278	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
279	
280	class AnalysisEntry(BaseModel):
281	    """Output from a single analysis tool execution with provenance tracking.
282	
283	    The key invariant: source_evidence_ids traces BACK to original evidence,
284	    never to "POLARIS Analysis Toolkit". Every [CITE:ev_xxx] token in
285	    markdown points to a real source URL in the bibliography.
286	    """
287	
288	    entry_id: str = Field(description="Unique ID, e.g. 'analysis_a1b2c3d4'")
289	    analysis_type: str = Field(
290	        description="Tool that produced this: extract_numeric_data, statistical_summary, etc.",
291	    )
292	    title: str = Field(description="Brief description of what this analysis shows")
293	    markdown: str = Field(
294	        description="Formatted results with [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens inline",
295	    )
296	    source_evidence_ids: list[str] = Field(
297	        description="Original evidence IDs that produced this analysis (provenance chain)",
298	        default_factory=list,
299	    )
300	    statistics: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)
301	    image_base64: str = Field(default="", description="Base64 PNG chart if any")
302	    insights: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
303	
304	
305	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
306	# Phase 5: ASSEMBLE output (final result)
307	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
308	
309	class V3ResultOutput(BaseModel):
310	    """Output JSON written to outputs/polaris_graph/{vector_id}.json.
311	
312	    Must contain all fields that live_server.py endpoints read.
313	    Validated against actual v1 result JSON for compatibility.
314	    """
315	
316	    vector_id: str
317	    original_query: str
318	    status: str = Field(description="completed|partial|failed")
319	    final_report: str = Field(description="Full markdown report")
320	    bibliography: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)

328	
329	    # v3-specific (frontend ignores unknown keys)
330	    v3_metadata: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)
331	
332	
333	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
334	# Trace event contract
335	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
336	
337	REQUIRED_TRACE_EVENTS = {
338	    "pipeline_start": ["query", "vector_id"],
339	    "pipeline_end": ["status", "total_words", "total_citations", "total_cost_usd", "elapsed_seconds"],
340	    "node_start": ["node"],

1	"""
2	Real-time Rich terminal dashboard for polaris graph pipeline.
3	
4	Displays live progress during pipeline execution using Rich library.
5	Updates on every astream() event from LangGraph.
6	"""
7	
8	import logging
9	import time
10	from typing import Any, Optional
11	
12	from rich.console import Console
13	from rich.live import Live
14	from rich.panel import Panel
15	from rich.table import Table
16	from rich.text import Text
17	
18	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
19	
20	
21	class PipelineDashboard:
22	    """Real-time terminal dashboard for polaris graph runs.
23	
24	    Usage:
25	        dashboard = PipelineDashboard(vector_id="V001", budget=150.0)
26	        with dashboard:
27	            async for event in app.astream(state, ...):
28	                dashboard.update_from_event(event)
29	    """
30	
31	    # Node display order and pretty names
32	    NODE_ORDER = [
33	        "plan", "search", "storm_interviews", "analyze", "verify",
34	        "evaluate", "synthesize", "search_gaps",
35	    ]
36	    NODE_LABELS = {
37	        "plan": "Plan",
38	        "search": "Search",
39	        "storm_interviews": "STORM",
40	        "analyze": "Analyze",
41	        "verify": "Verify",
42	        "evaluate": "Evaluate",
43	        "synthesize": "Synthesize",
44	        "search_gaps": "Gap Search",
45	    }
46	
47	    def __init__(
48	        self,
49	        vector_id: str = "",
50	        budget: float = 150.0,
51	        console: Optional[Console] = None,
52	    ):
53	        self.vector_id = vector_id
54	        self.budget = budget
55	        self.console = console or Console()
56	        self._live: Optional[Live] = None
57	        self._start_time = time.monotonic()
58	
59	        # State tracking
60	        self._current_node: str = ""
61	        self._completed_nodes: set[str] = set()
62	        self._node_details: dict[str, str] = {}
63	        self._iteration: int = 0
64	        self._cost: float = 0.0
65	        self._evidence_count: int = 0
66	        self._claims_count: int = 0
67	        self._faithfulness: float = -1.0
68	        self._word_count: int = 0
69	        self._citation_count: int = 0
70	        self._source_count: int = 0
71	        self._status: str = "initializing"
72	        self._custom_messages: list[str] = []
73	        self._search_details: dict[str, int] = {}
74	
75	    def __enter__(self):
76	        self._start_time = time.monotonic()
77	        self._live = Live(
78	            self._build_display(),
79	            console=self.console,
80	            refresh_per_second=2,
81	            transient=False,
82	        )
83	        self._live.__enter__()
84	        return self
85	
86	    def __exit__(self, *args):
87	        if self._live:
88	            self._live.__exit__(*args)
89	
90	    def update_from_event(self, event: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
91	        """Update dashboard from an astream() event.
92	
93	        astream(stream_mode="updates") yields dicts like:
94	        {"node_name": {"key": value, ...}}
95	
96	        astream(stream_mode=["updates", "custom"]) can also yield
97	        custom writer events.
98	        """
99	        if not event or not isinstance(event, dict):
100	            return
101	
102	        for node_name, node_output in event.items():
103	            if not isinstance(node_output, dict):
104	                continue
105	
106	            # Track node completion
107	            self._completed_nodes.add(node_name)
108	            self._current_node = node_name
109	
110	            # Extract metrics from node output
111	            self._extract_metrics(node_name, node_output)
112	
113	        self._refresh()
114	
115	    def update_custom(
116	        self,
117	        phase: str = "",
118	        progress: int = 0,
119	        message: str = "",
120	        **kwargs,
121	    ) -> None:
122	        """Update from a custom stream writer event."""
123	        if phase:
124	            self._current_node = phase
125	        if message:
126	            self._custom_messages.append(message)
127	            # Keep last 5 messages
128	            if len(self._custom_messages) > 5:
129	                self._custom_messages = self._custom_messages[-5:]
130	        for key, value in kwargs.items():
131	            if key == "cost":
132	                self._cost = float(value)
133	            elif key == "evidence_count":
134	                self._evidence_count = int(value)
135	            elif key == "faithfulness":
136	                self._faithfulness = float(value)
137	        self._refresh()
138	
139	    def node_start(self, node_name: str, **details) -> None:
140	        """Mark a node as currently running."""
141	        self._current_node = node_name
142	        detail_parts = [f"{k}={v}" for k, v in details.items()]
143	        if detail_parts:
144	            self._node_details[node_name] = ", ".join(detail_parts)
145	        self._refresh()
146	
147	    def node_end(self, node_name: str, **details) -> None:
148	        """Mark a node as complete."""
149	        self._completed_nodes.add(node_name)
150	        detail_parts = [f"{k}={v}" for k, v in details.items()]
151	        if detail_parts:
152	            self._node_details[node_name] = ", ".join(detail_parts)
153	        self._refresh()
154	
155	    def _extract_metrics(self, node: str, output: dict) -> None:
156	        """Extract relevant metrics from node output."""
157	        # Evidence count
158	        if "evidence" in output:
159	            ev = output["evidence"]
160	            if isinstance(ev, list):
161	                self._evidence_count = len(ev)
162	
163	        # Claims
164	        if "claims" in output:
165	            claims = output["claims"]
166	            if isinstance(claims, list):
167	                self._claims_count = len(claims)
168	
169	        # Faithfulness
170	        if "faithfulness_score" in output:
171	            self._faithfulness = output["faithfulness_score"]
172	
173	        # Quality metrics
174	        qm = output.get("quality_metrics")
175	        if isinstance(qm, dict):
176	            self._word_count = qm.get("total_words", self._word_count)
177	            self._citation_count = qm.get("total_citations", self._citation_count)
178	            self._source_count = qm.get("unique_sources", self._source_count)
179	
180	        # LLM usage / cost
181	        usage = output.get("llm_usage")
182	        if isinstance(usage, dict):
183	            self._cost = usage.get("total_cost_usd", self._cost)
184	
185	        # Iteration
186	        if "iteration_count" in output:
187	            self._iteration = output["iteration_count"]
188	
189	        # Status
190	        if "status" in output:
191	            self._status = output["status"]
192	
193	        # Search details
194	        if node == "search":
195	            if "web_results" in output:
196	                wr = output["web_results"]
197	                self._search_details["web"] = len(wr) if isinstance(wr, list) else 0
198	            if "academic_results" in output:
199	                ar = output["academic_results"]
200	                self._search_details["academic"] = len(ar) if isinstance(ar, list) else 0
201	            if "agentic_search_rounds" in output:
202	                self._search_details["rounds"] = output["agentic_search_rounds"]
203	
204	    def _refresh(self) -> None:
205	        """Refresh the live display."""
206	        if self._live:
207	            self._live.update(self._build_display())
208	
209	    def _build_display(self) -> Panel:
210	        """Build the full dashboard panel."""
211	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - self._start_time
212	        minutes = int(elapsed // 60)
213	        seconds = int(elapsed % 60)
214	
215	        # Header
216	        header = f"POLARIS GRAPH -- {self.vector_id} -- LIVE"
217	
218	        # Node status table
219	        table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold cyan", expand=True)
220	        table.add_column("Phase", style="bold", width=14)
221	        table.add_column("Status", width=8, justify="center")
222	        table.add_column("Details", ratio=1)
223	
224	        for node in self.NODE_ORDER:
225	            label = self.NODE_LABELS.get(node, node)
226	            details = self._node_details.get(node, "")
227	
228	            if node in self._completed_nodes and node != self._current_node:
229	                status = Text("DONE", style="bold green")
230	            elif node == self._current_node:
231	                status = Text("LIVE", style="bold yellow")
232	            else:
233	                status = Text("--", style="dim")
234	
235	            # Add search sub-details
236	            if node == "search" and self._search_details:
237	                parts = []
238	                if "web" in self._search_details:
239	                    parts.append(f"web={self._search_details['web']}")
240	                if "academic" in self._search_details:
241	                    parts.append(f"acad={self._search_details['academic']}")
242	                if "rounds" in self._search_details:
243	                    parts.append(f"rounds={self._search_details['rounds']}")
244	                if parts:
245	                    details = ", ".join(parts)
246	
247	            table.add_row(label, status, details)
248	
249	        # Metrics row
250	        metrics_table = Table(show_header=False, expand=True, box=None)
251	        metrics_table.add_column(ratio=1)
252	        metrics_table.add_column(ratio=1)
253	        metrics_table.add_column(ratio=1)
254	        metrics_table.add_column(ratio=1)
255	
256	        faith_str = f"{self._faithfulness:.1%}" if self._faithfulness >= 0 else "N/A"
257	        budget_remaining = self.budget - self._cost
258	
259	        metrics_table.add_row(
260	            f"Evidence: {self._evidence_count}",
261	            f"Claims: {self._claims_count}",
262	            f"Faith: {faith_str}",
263	            f"Iter: {self._iteration}",
264	        )
265	        metrics_table.add_row(
266	            f"Words: {self._word_count:,}",
267	            f"Citations: {self._citation_count}",
268	            f"Sources: {self._source_count}",
269	            f"Status: {self._status}",
270	        )
271	
272	        # Footer
273	        footer_table = Table(show_header=False, expand=True, box=None)
274	        footer_table.add_column(ratio=1)
275	        footer_table.add_column(ratio=1)
276	        footer_table.add_column(ratio=1)
277	        footer_table.add_row(
278	            f"Cost: ${self._cost:.2f}",
279	            f"Time: {minutes}:{seconds:02d}",
280	            f"Budget: ${budget_remaining:.2f} remaining",
281	        )
282	
283	        # Custom messages
284	        msg_text = ""
285	        if self._custom_messages:
286	            msg_text = "\n".join(f"  > {m}" for m in self._custom_messages[-3:])
287	
288	        # Compose
289	        from rich.console import Group
290	        parts = [table, Text(""), metrics_table]
291	        if msg_text:
292	            parts.append(Text(msg_text, style="dim italic"))
293	        parts.append(Text(""))
294	        parts.append(footer_table)
295	
296	        return Panel(
297	            Group(*parts),
298	            title=header,
299	            border_style="bright_blue",
300	        )
